data.quality: no relationship — she is an object of the deed
data.power_dynamic: total asymmetry
data.register_shift: does not address her at all; speaks the formula, then strikes

data.quality: Distant, formal, transactional respect across an unbridgeable cultural line
data.power_dynamic: Rosen high in the past tense; in present tense, a faded credential Hadji Murad invokes
data.speech_register_shift: Switches to slow, ceremonial Russian — the register of granting rank — and avoids first-person warmth

data.type: Successor in command of the Caucasus
data.quality: Professional courtesy masking a generational handover Rosen resents
data.power_dynamic: Vorontsov institutionally ascendant; Rosen's authority survives only as precedent
data.speech_register_shift: Becomes more formally correct, almost stilted, addressing Vorontsov by full title

data.quality: Loyal subordinate of an autocrat he has never personally questioned
data.power_dynamic: Total deference
data.speech_register_shift: Court register; eyes lowered in reported speech; no opinion offered unless asked

data.quality: Adversary respected as a problem of state, not as a man
data.power_dynamic: Mutually unyielding; neither has met the other
data.speech_register_shift: Names him only as 'the Imam' — refusing the personal name as a small administrative violence

data.type: Master / sworn murid; surrogate father-son bond layered over military discipleship
data.quality: Absolute, wordless devotion; the central relationship of Eldar's life and the lens through which he reads every other person.
data.power_dynamic: Total subordination — HM commands by glance; Eldar obeys before the sentence ends. Eldar's only sphere of independent judgement is the threat assessment of strangers.
data.speech_register_shift: Lowest, softest, most clipped register; eyes down, voice barely above the room. Religious vocatives ('Agha') used naturally.

data.quality: Wordless solidarity; they divide the watch without negotiation. No rivalry, no warmth — a working trust between two silent men.
data.power_dynamic: Roughly equal among the murids; Eldar slightly senior in HM's intimate orbit.
data.speech_register_shift: Even more compressed than with HM — often pure gesture; when he speaks, it is operational ('You take the door.').

data.type: Brother-murid, but a different temperament — Khan Mahoma talks, Eldar does not
data.quality: Quiet tolerance; Eldar registers Khan Mahoma's volubility as a small risk to be managed near HM.
data.power_dynamic: Equal in rank; Eldar's stillness exerts a subtle disciplinary pressure on the others.
data.speech_register_shift: Curt, almost monosyllabic; refuses to be drawn into banter.

data.type: Brother-murid, senior in years and harshness
data.quality: Respect without affection; Eldar accepts Gamzalo's grimness as a model of the discipline he himself embodies more softly.
data.power_dynamic: Gamzalo slightly above by age and severity; Eldar defers in logistics, never in the matter of HM's personal safety.
data.speech_register_shift: Formal, brief, deferential where pragmatic; immovable when HM is the subject.

data.type: Hosts / captors / strangers across a cultural and political line
data.quality: Wary courtesy; he treats them as potential threats in fine clothing and never relaxes his body in their presence.
data.power_dynamic: Nominally below them inside the fort; in his own moral hierarchy they are guests in HM's orbit, not the other way round.
data.speech_register_shift: Almost mute; answers only direct questions, in the shortest grammatically complete form, never volunteering.

character_relationships.to: Maria Dmitrievna and the women of the fort

data.type: Outsiders to the murid bond; alien gender register
data.quality: Respectful distance; he lowers his eyes further, will not be alone with them, treats kindness as a complication rather than a gift.
data.power_dynamic: Asymmetric and culturally coded — he gives way physically but does not adopt their idiom.
data.speech_register_shift: Reduced almost to gesture; a small bow of the head replaces most sentences.

data.type: host / fascinated witness — Russian officer to Avar naib
data.quality: respectful, slightly awed, quietly envious of HM's wholeness; the relationship is the moral mirror of the screenplay
data.power_dynamic: officially Butler is the host and HM the guest-prisoner; emotionally HM is the senior man, and Butler knows it
data.register_shift: drops the bright Petersburg manner; sentences shorten, become more formal and more sincere; uses learned Tartar greetings

data.type: billet-comrade and friend — fellow line officer, married to Marya Dmitrievna
data.quality: warm, loyal, faintly guilty (because of Marya Dmitrievna); the relationship anchors Butler's restraint
data.power_dynamic: rough equality of rank; Petrov is the steadier, plainer man, Butler the more polished
data.register_shift: easy, fraternal, full of 'my dear fellow' and shared silences; obscenity still avoided

data.type: comrade's mistress / unspoken object of attraction
data.quality: tender, courteous, deliberately fraternal; Butler maintains 'a simple, respectful tone' precisely because he feels otherwise
data.power_dynamic: she has the moral authority of the household; Butler defers
data.register_shift: exaggerated courtesy, French politesse, careful eye-contact — the manners covering desire

data.type: subordinate / junior officer to garrison superior
data.quality: outwardly correct, inwardly contemptuous of their drunkenness and brutality, though he shares both
data.power_dynamic: clearly subordinate; obeys, salutes, drinks when ordered
data.register_shift: stiffer, more formulaic, regimental phrases, no irony allowed to surface

data.quality: buoyant, anecdotal, performatively cheerful — the public face of the campaign
data.power_dynamic: rough equality; Butler is slightly senior in style by virtue of his Guards background
data.register_shift: bright, quick, full of campaign slang and song-quotations

character_relationships.to: the Chechens / villagers of the burned aoul

data.quality: almost no direct speech to them; the relationship is one of guilty witness rather than dialogue
data.power_dynamic: absolute military dominance, moral inversion
data.register_shift: silence, or curt orders relayed through NCOs; the brightness drains from his voice

data.quality: affectionate, slightly maternal, conspiratorial; she is his unacknowledged co-administrator of Tiflis society
data.power_dynamic: publicly his consort, privately his equal — she edits him in French while he speaks Russian
data.register_shift: in private: faster, more familiar, French diminutives, dry edits of his evening; in public: ceremonious, deferential, finishes his half-sentences only when they begin to falter

data.type: hostess and exotic guest; an unexpected, unspoken fascination
data.quality: warm, curious, slightly disturbed; he is the first guest she cannot fully translate
data.power_dynamic: she is the social superior in her own house and yet, in his presence, briefly the supplicant — the language barrier inverts her usual advantage
data.register_shift: speaks more slowly, drops the irony, prefers French even though he understands neither French nor Russian; the lightness becomes a kind of formality

data.quality: indulgent, flattering, faintly bored
data.power_dynamic: absolute within the drawing-room; she decides who is interesting this season
data.register_shift: fluid French-Russian code-switching, abundant endearments, tag questions that disguise commands as invitations

data.type: (post-fusion) the same role — she now also delivers the single intervention that interrupts a general's tactless remark at the Tiflis dinner
data.quality: social-correction by elegance, never by reproach
data.power_dynamic: her interruption is unanswerable because it is phrased as concern for the guest's comfort
data.register_shift: from drawing-room banter to one short, perfectly weighted French sentence that closes the subject

data.quality: Loyal, exasperated, maternal; built on shared loneliness rather than romance
data.power_dynamic: He has the rank and the legal standing; she has the daily authority — over the food, the drink, the household, and, quietly, over his dignity
data.speech_register_shift: Drops to the most informal register, mixes scolding with endearments ('batyushka', 'you fool'); uses imperatives freely

data.type: Hostess to a guest who is also a political prisoner of sorts
data.quality: Respectful, curious, increasingly sympathetic; she treats him as a man before she treats him as an enemy
data.power_dynamic: Formally she is the Russian woman of the house and he the surrendered chieftain, but morally she comes to feel he is the more upright of the two
data.speech_register_shift: More careful, slightly formal; fewer scolding tics; uses 'batyushka' with real respect rather than irony

data.type: Reluctant hostess to a foreign retainer
data.quality: Friction — cultural and domestic; they quarrel over the running of the household
data.power_dynamic: She runs the house, he answers only to Hadji Murad; neither will fully yield
data.speech_register_shift: Sharper, more clipped; orders rather than requests; no endearments

data.type: Companion / informal protector after Petrov's decline
data.quality: Affectionate but disillusioned; he is the witness to her final moral outburst
data.power_dynamic: She is older in experience of the frontier; he in rank — in practice they meet as equals in disenchantment
data.speech_register_shift: Plain, confiding, occasionally tender; she allows herself the rare 'you know' and the rare admission

character_relationships.to: Russian officers parading Hadji Murad's head

data.quality: Open contempt; they are the men her loyalty was supposed to extend to and no longer can
data.power_dynamic: They have the trophy, the rank, and the drink; she has only her voice — and uses it
data.speech_register_shift: Flat, hard, no endearments, no religious softening; the only time in the story she calls Russian soldiers what she thinks they are

data.quality: Loyal, professional, mutually respectful; Vorontsov genuinely trusts him with the most delicate Caucasian dossier.
data.power_dynamic: Strongly asymmetric — Vorontsov holds rank, class, and ear of the Tsar; Loris-Melikov holds linguistic and cultural access Vorontsov cannot replace.
data.speech_register_shift: Most formal Russian register; full titles, conditional constructions, never first-name. Voice quiets; sentences shorten.

data.type: Official handler / recorder of his life story; de facto confidant
data.quality: Professionally correct, privately respectful; the only Russian-side figure Hadji Murat speaks to without an interpreter between them.
data.power_dynamic: Officially Loris-Melikov controls access, schedule, and how HM's words reach the Russians; in private the moral weight tilts to Hadji Murat, and Loris-Melikov knows it.
data.speech_register_shift: Switches into Tatar; rhythm slows, formality drops a degree, occasional 'brother'; questions become open rather than directive.

data.type: Aide to the Prince's household; social facilitator
data.quality: Courtly, deferential, useful to her in arranging Hadji Murat's presentation at receptions.
data.power_dynamic: She outranks him socially; he is the discreet functionary who makes her salons work.
data.speech_register_shift: Court-French politeness layered on Russian; lighter, more decorative phrasing; never mentions intelligence work in her presence.

data.quality: Cool, correct; they suspect his Caucasian sympathies, he suspects their dispatches misrepresent the field.
data.power_dynamic: They have institutional weight; he has Vorontsov's confidence and the actual transcripts.
data.speech_register_shift: Stiffened, maximally formal; declarative sentences, no Tatar idioms allowed to surface, even in passing.

data.type: Functional duplicate within the same scene-space (Tatar-speaking trusted intermediary)
data.quality: Redundant — same dramatic role, fewer scenes; in this adaptation his narrative function is absorbed by Loris-Melikov.
data.power_dynamic: N/A in feature: Tarkhanov collapses into Loris-Melikov per fusion logic.
data.speech_register_shift: N/A — Tarkhanov is fused away; Loris-Melikov inherits any scene-essential beat.

data.type: Sworn blood-brother and murid; nursed by HM's mother Patimat in the ritual that ended the feud between their families
data.quality: Reverential, total, almost filial — HM is master, brother, and saviour in one
data.power_dynamic: Khanefi absolutely subordinate; obeys without comment
data.speech_register_shift: Voice drops, addresses HM as 'agha'; sentences shorten; eyes lower

data.type: Fellow murid; closest peer in HM's inner circle
data.quality: Wordless solidarity; the two move as a single body around HM
data.power_dynamic: Equal, but Eldar is the active companion and Khanefi the household steward
data.speech_register_shift: Speaks Avar in clipped half-sentences; often communicates by gesture alone

data.type: Fellow murid; the warrior beside the steward
data.quality: Cool respect; each guards a different flank of HM's life
data.power_dynamic: Parallel functions; neither commands the other
data.speech_register_shift: Operational, terse — orders about horses, watches, ammunition

data.quality: Quiet disapproval of Khan Mahoma's frivolity; tolerates him for HM's sake
data.power_dynamic: Equal in rank; Khanefi the gravity to Khan Mahoma's lightness
data.speech_register_shift: Drier, shorter, refuses banter

data.quality: Mentoring without warmth — corrects, does not praise
data.power_dynamic: Senior; gives instructions about pack-horses and weapons
data.speech_register_shift: Imperative mode, shorter sentences

data.type: Russian woman at the fort who expelled him from her kitchen
data.quality: Combustible, near-violent — the only relationship in which his rage shows openly
data.power_dynamic: Khanefi denied agency in her domain, responds with the dagger he never draws on his own
data.speech_register_shift: Broken, percussive Russian; or no words, only the threat in his body

data.type: Foster-mother through the milk ritual; the woman who ended the blood feud by nursing him
data.quality: Sacred; the source of every loyalty he has
data.power_dynamic: Filial reverence — she is the reason he lives
data.speech_register_shift: Speaks her name only in invocation, never in narration

data.quality: devoted, playful, slightly worshipful; the youngest-feeling of the four murids
data.power_dynamic: fully subordinate; volunteers for the most exposed errands
data.speech_register_shift: drops the joking opener; voice tightens to clipped report-Russian/Avar; calls him only 'Hadji Murad', never a diminutive

data.quality: affectionate friction — the joker against the earnest one
data.power_dynamic: equal in rank, opposite in temperament
data.speech_register_shift: louder, more teasing; uses Khanefi as straight man for jokes about ritual and prayer

data.quality: protective, mocking-fond
data.power_dynamic: Khan Mahoma the worldlier, Eldar the more silent — Khan Mahoma fills Eldar's silences
data.speech_register_shift: softer, almost gentle; teases without sting

data.type: operational partner on the Vorontsov mission
data.quality: wary cooperation; insider murid vs. hired Chechen guide
data.power_dynamic: equal in the field but Khan Mahoma carries Hadji Murad's words and therefore the authority
data.speech_register_shift: terser, more transactional; fewer jokes, no diminutives

data.quality: performed deference masking sharp observation
data.power_dynamic: asymmetric — Khan Mahoma is the supplicant's mouth
data.speech_register_shift: broken Russian deliberately exaggerated; bows in word and posture; jokes withheld until safely outside the gates

data.quality: feared and unspoken
data.power_dynamic: Khan Mahoma's life and his master's family hang on Shamil's mood
data.speech_register_shift: name lowered to a near-whisper; jokes vanish entirely when Shamil is mentioned

data.type: Unconfessed romantic infatuation across a social barrier (she is married, her husband is his superior's circle)
data.quality: Tender, distracted, slightly absurd — observed by everyone except, he hopes, her
data.power_dynamic: She holds all of it; his rank cannot reach her station as hostess and married woman
data.speech_register_shift: Drops into his most polished, French-flecked, ironic-gallant register; sentences shorten and slow; never directly addresses feeling

data.type: Old comrade — fellow Cadet College alumnus, now both posted in the Caucasus
data.quality: Easy, fraternal, the shorthand of shared schooldays
data.power_dynamic: Roughly equal; mutual ribbing without status anxiety
data.speech_register_shift: Most relaxed register — schoolboy nicknames, half-finished sentences, mess-room laughter

data.quality: Paternal-cheerful in the Guards manner — courteous to NCOs, formally correct, not intimate
data.power_dynamic: Absolute authority, lightly worn
data.speech_register_shift: Drops the French and the irony; voice becomes clipped, declarative, audible at distance

data.type: Enemy commander turned object of fascinated Russian observation
data.quality: Curiosity tinged with the romance of the 'noble mountaineer' — Poltoratsky reads him through Petersburg eyes
data.power_dynamic: None directly; Poltoratsky is a witness, not an interlocutor
data.speech_register_shift: Speaks of him in the elevated, slightly theatrical register reserved for legends — the Caucasus made into a story he can tell back home

data.type: Subordinate to a circle he socially belongs to but militarily depends on
data.quality: Punctilious deference layered over salon familiarity
data.power_dynamic: They outrank him; he flatters without grovelling
data.speech_register_shift: Formal address, military correctness, the French tags retained as a class-marker rather than a flourish

data.quality: Total, unspoken loyalty; pre-verbal trust
data.power_dynamic: Subordinate by oath, equal in the moment of combat
data.register_shift: Speaks least of all, addresses him as 'Hadji Agha', never contradicts in front of others; in private will state a tactical disagreement in one flat sentence and then obey.

data.type: Fellow murid, comrade in HM's inner circle
data.quality: Functional brotherhood — household-keeper and warrior, two halves of one service
data.power_dynamic: Roughly equal; Gamzalo carries the harder edge
data.register_shift: Curt, practical, two-word exchanges about horses, watches, supplies; no warmth in language, full warmth in reliance.

data.quality: Quiet mentorship under fire; corrects by example, not lecture
data.power_dynamic: Gamzalo leads, Eldar follows
data.register_shift: Imperatives only — 'Take the left', 'Eyes up' — never explanations.

data.type: Hosts who are also captors in his eyes
data.quality: Cold civility on the surface; absolute distrust beneath
data.power_dynamic: Outwardly subordinate guest, inwardly an armed witness
data.register_shift: Speaks only when addressed, in the shortest possible answers, never volunteers; declines Russian forms of politeness.

data.quality: Reverence for the office, no comment on the man's quarrel with Hadji Murad
data.power_dynamic: Absent authority; Gamzalo refuses to weigh in
data.register_shift: Will not name Shamil in conversation if he can avoid it; if forced, uses the title only.

data.quality: Respect-based, professional loyalty that hardens into personal loyalty; chosen, not inherited
data.power_dynamic: Hadji Murad commands; Bata serves — but Bata's value lies in skills the leader cannot replace, giving him a quiet leverage
data.speech_register_shift: Drops boasting almost entirely with HM; speaks shorter, more direct, defers without grovelling — the only man around whom he stops performing

data.type: Mission partner; insider-murid paired with outsider-guide
data.quality: Functional alliance with quiet rivalry — they need each other but represent two different ways of belonging to Hadji Murad
data.power_dynamic: Roughly equal in mission terms; Khan Mahoma has rank-by-name, Bata has skill-by-terrain
data.speech_register_shift: Banter in Chechen/Avar, mountain humor, clipped jokes about who is leading whom; switches to formal cooperation when Russians are present

data.quality: Older-brother-on-the-road dynamic; Bata's authority is teacher-like without tenderness
data.power_dynamic: Bata leads, Eldar follows — Bata's experience outranks Eldar's loyalty
data.speech_register_shift: Instructional, terse, occasional dry humor; uses imperative more than with anyone else

data.quality: Wary, transactional; Bata performs harmlessness deliberately
data.power_dynamic: They hold the rifles; he holds the message — temporary parity through usefulness
data.speech_register_shift: Broken Russian, simplified syntax, repeats key words, drops all proverbs, exaggerates politeness ('friend', 'sir'); body language goes deliberately small

data.type: Fellow murid in the final stand
data.quality: Comrade-in-arms by circumstance; functional bond at the end
data.power_dynamic: Equal — both subordinate to Hadji Murad's last decisions
data.speech_register_shift: Almost wordless; the talkative guide goes quiet at the end, communicating in glances and small actions

data.quality: Asymmetric, sycophantic, transactional — Chernyshov serves Nicholas's vanity in exchange for protection and standing.
data.power_dynamic: Absolute imbalance: Nicholas dictates, Chernyshov decodes and confirms.
data.speech_register_shift: Maximum deference; French ornamentation, third-person address ('your Imperial Majesty'); never initiates, only responds; tone modulates to mirror the Tsar's humor.

data.type: Bureaucratic rival; co-minister of imperial machinery
data.quality: Cold professional courtesy in writing (French letters), private resentment and undermining at court — envies Vorontsov's stature in the Caucasus and the respect he commands.
data.power_dynamic: Roughly equal in formal rank, but Chernyshov holds the proximity-to-Tsar advantage and weaponizes it.
data.speech_register_shift: In correspondence: elaborate court French, elaborate respect ('mon cher Prince'); when reporting on him to Nicholas: even-toned, factual, with strategically chosen omissions and emphases.

data.type: Junior ministerial colleague / portfolio assistant in the Nicholas-cabinet scenes
data.quality: Functional, hierarchical; Dolgoruky carries papers, Chernyshov carries the agenda.
data.power_dynamic: Chernyshov is senior; Dolgoruky defers.
data.speech_register_shift: Brisk, instructive, slightly impatient — the only register in which Chernyshov permits himself mild authority.

data.type: Subject of dispatches; never met in person
data.quality: Abstract — a name on a report, a problem to be administered; no human dimension granted.
data.power_dynamic: Total bureaucratic distance; Chernyshov holds the pen, Hadji Murat is ink.
data.speech_register_shift: Reduces him to administrative noun-phrases ('the Avar', 'the deserter', 'the prisoner under guard at Nukha').

data.quality: Deep, unstated loyalty masked as gruff teasing; Panov is the witness to Avdeev's quiet sacrifice and his death
data.power_dynamic: Panov is older, NCO, the steady one; Avdeev is the soft-hearted private. Panov leads, but Avdeev's death exposes that the moral weight sits with the private, not the NCO.
data.register_shift: Drops from camp-banter and orders into near-whispered diminutives (Avdeyushka) and one-word reassurances at the deathbed.

data.quality: Respectful, professional; Panov is the experienced enlisted anchor who quietly informs the young officer about the field — including identifying Shamil's banner.
data.power_dynamic: Officer/NCO formal hierarchy; in practice Panov knows the ground better and Butler knows it.
data.register_shift: Tightens to clipped, deferential 'your honour' register; shorter sentences, no proverbs, pipe out of mouth.

character_relationships.to: Soldiers under his command (Petrukha, Bondarenko, Nikitin, Serogin in the source roster)

data.quality: Paternal-fraternal; mediates their squabbles, indulges their jokes, draws the line at carelessness on watch.
data.power_dynamic: Quiet authority by experience rather than rank-fetish; obeyed because he is right, not because he shouts.
data.register_shift: Uses diminutives and proverbs freely; teases; sharpens to flat imperatives only when discipline or safety demands it.

data.quality: Wary professional curiosity; Panov reads Hadji Murat the way he reads weather — as a fact of the field, not a moral question.
data.power_dynamic: None direct; Panov is line-soldier, HM is the legend at the officers' table.
data.register_shift: Speaks of him only in third person, dryly, to other enlisted men; never to his face.

data.quality: Warm, unequal — Panov calls him 'smart chap'; Avdeev accepts the nickname with quiet pleasure
data.power_dynamic: Panov senior in experience; Avdeev defers easily, without resentment
data.speech_register_shift: Slightly more clipped and soldierly around Panov; fewer half-sentences, more 'yes, brother'

data.quality: Easy, joking, mutually protective; the only social world he has in the Caucasus
data.power_dynamic: Equal; he is the cheerful one others lean on for small repairs and small laughs
data.speech_register_shift: Loosest register here — village idioms, jokes, complaints about food and weather

data.type: Family — the brother whose lot he took
data.quality: Loved, missed, faintly resented in a way he will not name
data.power_dynamic: Avdeev gave up his life for the brother's; the debt runs in both directions and neither speaks of it
data.speech_register_shift: Softer, slower, more rural — when he speaks of him, the soldier-slang drops away

data.type: Family — distant, barely known after years of service
data.quality: Tender, abstract, almost imagined; she exists for him as a direction more than a person
data.power_dynamic: Absent; the relationship survives on letters and memory
data.speech_register_shift: Quietest register — short sentences, no jokes, often a question he does not finish

data.quality: Polite, opaque; he performs the cheerful soldier they want to see
data.power_dynamic: Total — they name him only when he dies, and then only as a statistic
data.speech_register_shift: Stiff, clipped, all 'yes sir' and 'no sir'; the real Avdeev disappears entirely

data.type: Structural — the system that owns his life
data.quality: Unseen, unanswerable; he never addresses it directly
data.power_dynamic: He is a unit; it is a machine — the gap between 'Avdeev' and 'one private killed' is the whole point of his arc
data.speech_register_shift: He never speaks to it; when it is named near him, he goes quiet

data.type: Father–son; the relational engine of HM's entire defection arc
data.quality: Worshipful, wounded, unfinished — Yusuf adores a father he has barely had time to be known by, and that incompleteness is the wound the story turns on.
data.power_dynamic: Total asymmetry: HM is the actor in the world above; Yusuf is the stake in the pit below. Yusuf has only one power — to be loved enough to be saved.
data.speech_register_shift: With or about his father, Yusuf shifts into a slower, more formal Avar, borrowing his father's measured cadences; this is the only register in which his sentences reliably finish.

data.type: Captor / sovereign judge; the man who holds his life in his hand
data.quality: Fear masked as defiance; Yusuf knows Shamil is not a monster, which makes the verdict harder to bear.
data.power_dynamic: Absolute — Shamil decides whether he lives, dies, or is mutilated as a sign.
data.speech_register_shift: In Shamil's presence (or imagined presence) Yusuf's voice tightens into formal, pious phrasing; behind his back it collapses into curses and bravado.

data.type: Mother; the private self he protects from his public bravery
data.quality: Tender, almost unspoken; she is the name he will not say in front of guards.
data.power_dynamic: Emotional — she has no power to free him, but her grief is the one judgment he cannot bear.
data.speech_register_shift: Softer, simpler diction; sentences shorten and trail off; the only register in which he comes close to admitting fear.

data.type: Captors of his own people; ambivalent peers
data.quality: Contemptuous on the surface, kin underneath — they are Chechens and Avars like him, doing a job he might one day have done.
data.power_dynamic: Day-to-day power over food, light, movement; long-term they are interchangeable instruments of Shamil's will.
data.speech_register_shift: Sharper, more dialectal, full of insults and proverbs; uses shared idiom to remind them — and himself — that he is one of them, not a Russian's son.

data.type: Distant power his father has run to; both possible saviors and historic enemies
data.quality: Suspicion braided with hope; he hates needing them.
data.power_dynamic: Abstract — he never meets them on the page, but his fate is being negotiated in their language.
data.speech_register_shift: Refuses Russian vocabulary entirely; speaks of them in the third person plural, never by name, as if naming them would make the bargain real.

data.quality: lethal, asymmetric — Akhmet Khan envies HM's charisma and military reputation; HM regards Akhmet Khan as a liar whose denunciation cost him his standing with the Russians and forced him to Shamil
data.power_dynamic: Akhmet Khan holds the bureaucratic ear of the Russian generals; HM holds the loyalty of the mountain warriors — cold equilibrium that Akhmet breaks first by calumny
data.speech_register_shift: Refuses to address HM directly — speaks of him in the third person even within earshot; with HM absent, speech becomes more clinical and precise

data.quality: instrumental — Klugenau is the conduit through which Akhmet Khan converts personal hatred into Russian state action against HM
data.power_dynamic: subordinate in form, manipulator in fact — feeds Klugenau the framing that triggers HM's arrest
data.speech_register_shift: stiff Russian, exaggerated deference, every accusation framed as service to the Tsar

data.quality: humiliation source — the Khansha refused Sultanetta's hand for Akhmet Khan's son, which he believes HM engineered
data.power_dynamic: outranked socially by the Khansha's lineage; the refusal is the wound that festers into the denunciation of HM
data.speech_register_shift: in their presence, exaggerated courtly formality that barely masks contempt

character_relationships.to: His son (rides with Hadji Aga in final assault)

data.quality: the inheritance is not land but the unfinished feud with HM
data.power_dynamic: father instructs in absentia — the son's presence at HM's killing closes a circle the father opened
data.speech_register_shift: instructional, paternal — the only register in which Akhmet Khan permits warmth

data.quality: Akhmet Khan represents the Russian-collaborating khan; Shamil represents the resisting imam — Akhmet uses Shamil's name as the ultimate accusation against HM
data.power_dynamic: no direct contact in text; weaponizes Shamil-association as a denunciation tool
data.speech_register_shift: speaks Shamil's name with theatrical disgust, especially when Russian officers can hear

data.quality: Reverent, protective, unspoken brotherhood forged by code rather than history
data.power_dynamic: Sado yields the deference of a host to a great warrior, yet inside his own walls his moral authority is absolute
data.speech_register_shift: Drops to near-whisper; speech becomes more formal, more invocational; addresses HM by full name with the honorific weight of prayer

data.type: Distant spiritual sovereign whose order he defies
data.quality: Outward reverence, inward refusal — the Imam commands his faith but not his hearth
data.power_dynamic: Subject to Shamil's religious-political authority, yet sovereign within his own threshold
data.speech_register_shift: Names Shamil only with the title 'the Imam'; never argues against him aloud, only acts against him in silence

character_relationships.to: Sado's Household (fused: Father, Mother, Wife, Son)

data.type: Household elders and dependents now consolidated into Sado's single voice
data.quality: Sado carries their welcome, their warning, and their farewell himself — the household speaks through him
data.power_dynamic: Sole spokesman for his roof; household presence becomes silent backdrop, gestures absorbed into his actions
data.speech_register_shift: When delivering what was once Father's proverb or Mother's blessing, Sado's cadence slows further and takes on the formal weight of inherited speech

data.quality: Bound by blood and custom, yet he places kunak code above their immediate safety
data.power_dynamic: He is one of the elders they listen to, yet he knows they may turn on him for this night's choice
data.speech_register_shift: Public speech becomes terser, more proverbial, never explanatory — he will not justify a kunak's sheltering to anyone

data.quality: domineering — she rules him through shame, not affection
data.power_dynamic: she dominant; her tongue is the weapon that sends him to his death
data.speech_register_shift: shortens, hardens, and formalizes — he addresses her as 'Mother' but speaks as a subject before a queen

data.quality: trusting affection beneath rank; HM warns him and is heard but not heeded
data.power_dynamic: nominally Abu Nutsal is higher (Khan's son), but HM is the more capable warrior — an inverted hierarchy of rank and competence
data.speech_register_shift: softens; uses 'brother'; allows the only flickers of doubt he ever voices

data.quality: warm, boyish, the one bond untouched by politics
data.power_dynamic: equal — the only relation in which he is neither sovereign nor son
data.speech_register_shift: informal, playful, occasionally fragmentary

data.quality: fraternal solidarity shadowed by shared fate
data.power_dynamic: he as eldest carries seniority; in the trap they share it equally
data.speech_register_shift: elder-brother tone — instructive, protective, terse

data.quality: lethal — Hamzad is the trap into which Abu Nutsal walks
data.power_dynamic: Hamzad dominant through deception; Abu Nutsal walks into it believing himself the equal
data.speech_register_shift: would speak with cold formality, the language of summit between rivals — but in the source he never reaches the meeting alive in dialogue

data.type: Foster-brother (nursed by HM's mother), sworn brother of youth
data.quality: Tender, dependent — Umma looks to HM for the will he himself lacks
data.power_dynamic: HM is the moral and martial elder despite shared age; Umma defers
data.register_shift: Drops all ceremony; uses 'brother' and the diminutive of HM's name; sentences shorten further — fragments of trust

data.quality: Loyal, shadowing — moves where Abu Nutsal moves
data.power_dynamic: Subordinate; never contradicts
data.register_shift: Echoes Abu Nutsal's phrasing almost verbatim

data.type: Mother and political principal of the Khanate
data.quality: Reverent, infantilised — she is his final authority
data.power_dynamic: Total maternal/political dominance over him
data.register_shift: Most formal register; full courtly invocations; never argues

data.type: Murshid / Imam-rival who lures the brothers to their deaths
data.quality: Outwardly respectful, inwardly terrified — Umma cannot read the trap
data.power_dynamic: Hamzad dominates spiritually and rhetorically; Umma is prey
data.register_shift: Religious formulae intensify; sentences grow shorter and more agreeing

data.quality: Flattered, drunk, ashamed — and drawn back for more
data.power_dynamic: They patronise; he submits in exchange for praise
data.register_shift: Tries to mimic their swagger; lineage-boasts replace courtly Avar formulae; rhythm becomes uneven

data.type: Nominal subordinate / actual rival in influence
data.quality: Cold-correct, surface-cordial, undercut by jurisdictional resentment
data.power_dynamic: Meller-Zakomelsky outranks formally; Vorontsov outweighs him in court influence — a humiliation Meller-Zakomelsky must absorb in protocol-language
data.speech_register_shift: Formal third-person address, French phrases borrowed from court register; never the warm familiarity Vorontsov uses with peers

data.quality: Procedural, depersonalized; treats him as a jurisdictional question, not a man
data.power_dynamic: Total formal authority, zero personal interest
data.speech_register_shift: Speaks about him in the third person even when present; uses interpreter rather than direct address

data.quality: Strictly hierarchical; the aide is voice and conduit, not interlocutor
data.power_dynamic: Absolute
data.speech_register_shift: Curt imperatives; no pleasantries

data.type: Junior officer who 'erred' by not reporting first
data.quality: Reproachful but ultimately deferential to the family name
data.power_dynamic: Formal superior who must let the matter pass once the father intervenes
data.speech_register_shift: Reproof framed as procedural correction, not personal anger

data.quality: Warm, gruff, paternal; Butler lives under his roof and rides beside him
data.power_dynamic: Petrov is clearly above in rank and in domestic authority, but Butler's youth and Guards background give him a moral leverage Petrov half-resents and half-relies on
data.speech_register_shift: With Butler alone: more relaxed, near-confessional in fragments; in front of other officers: clipped, commanding, performs the senior role

data.quality: Long-eroded affection encased in routine; she is the caretaker of his dissolution
data.power_dynamic: Public: he commands; private: she manages him, his drinking, the household, and increasingly the fort's social fabric — a quiet inversion
data.speech_register_shift: In public: terse, almost formal, as if she were a fellow officer; in private: shorter still, often only a name, a sigh, a single imperative

data.quality: Bonded by ritual — patrol, song, vodka — not by intimacy
data.power_dynamic: Unquestioned authority on duty; on drinking nights the hierarchy dissolves into shared chaos that he leads from the front
data.speech_register_shift: Duty: imperatives, surnames, no warmth; drink: 'brothers,' first names, toasts, embraces

character_relationships.to: Hadji Murat (if encountered in fort scenes)

data.type: Distant counterpart — the famed Avar guest in the Russian sector
data.quality: Curiosity tempered by professional wariness; Petrov registers HM as a phenomenon, not a peer
data.power_dynamic: Formally Petrov is host and Russian authority; in human stature HM dwarfs him, which Petrov senses and covers with extra brusqueness
data.speech_register_shift: Stiffer, more formal, fewer tics; the drunken Petrov never appears in HM's presence

locations.description: An agricultural landscape of hay-fields, rye, wildflowers, and a ploughed black-earth field. At its center: the battered crimson thistle — the film's thematic emblem. This frame-narrative location anchors Tolstoi's authorial voice before the story proper begins.

production_design.physical_signature: Solitary crimson thistle crushed against black ploughed earth
production_design.color_palette: Saturated greens, black earth, single crimson accent
production_design.atmosphere: Still pastoral beauty masking latent violence
production_design.soundscape: Wind through rye, distant cart, silence

locations.description: A hillside Chechen mountain village of interconnected saklyas — clay-plastered, whitewashed interiors, weapons on nails, earthen floors, rooftop sleeping platforms. Domestically intimate yet martial. This is where Hadji Murad takes his first refuge after fleeing Shamil, and where Russian punitive raids later reduce it to ash.

production_design.physical_signature: Whitewashed clay saklya walls hung with polished weapons and gleaming basins
production_design.atmosphere: Oppressive intimacy beneath a code of honour
production_design.soundscape: Kizyak smoke, distant cattle, hushed conspiratorial voices, wind off mountain

locations.canonical_name: Caucasus Wilderness — Forest, Ravine & Escape Routes
locations.description: The forested ravines, hidden glades, river bends, and night-shrouded mountain paths that form the contested no-man's-land between Chechen villages and Russian forts. Hadji Murad moves through this darkness with his murids — a world of hobbled horses, doused bonfires, rushing water, and jackals.

production_design.physical_signature: Dense plane-tree forest at night, firelight briefly visible then extinguished
production_design.color_palette: Near-black deep greens, silver moonlight, amber bonfire flash
production_design.atmosphere: Hunted fugitive tension in absolute darkness
production_design.soundscape: Rushing water, wind, jackal howl, muffled hoofbeats

locations.description: An advanced Russian military fort on the Chechen frontier — barracks, a hospital ward, the gate opening onto darkness, and the best house (Vorontsov's) still lit while soldiers sleep. The gate at Shahgirinsk is the threshold between imperial order and Chechen wilderness. Night ambush outposts extend into the surrounding forest.

production_design.physical_signature: Timber palisade gate opening into blackness; one lit window amid dark barracks
production_design.color_palette: Ash-grey timber, yellow candlelight, blue-white snow
production_design.atmosphere: Frontier anxiety masked by routine military order
production_design.soundscape: Boot steps on frozen ground, distant rifle shot, wind

locations.description: The most luxurious dwelling inside the frontier fort — carpeted floors, rich curtains, a four-candle card table, armchairs, a child playing on the floor. The collision of Hadji Murad's mountain world with Russian imperial domesticity happens here, in a room that shouldn't exist at a war's edge.

production_design.physical_signature: Four-candle card table throwing warm light on carpeted floor inside timber-walled fort room
production_design.color_palette: Warm amber, deep red carpet, pale curtain linen
production_design.atmosphere: Imperial privilege as absurd garrison theatre
production_design.soundscape: Clock ticking, muffled wind outside, polite conversation

locations.description: The forested ravine edge where Russian infantry holds a skirmish line against Chechen fighters — open descending slope, tree cover, ravine below. The location where Avdeev is fatally wounded. Operationally contiguous with Vozvizhensk but cinematically distinct: this is where the war is actually felt.

production_design.physical_signature: Soldiers prone behind forest treeline, bare slope falling into ravine below
production_design.color_palette: Drab olive, steel-grey bark, dirty snow, blood red
production_design.atmosphere: Low-intensity dread interrupted by sudden violence
production_design.soundscape: Rifle crack, distant Chechen shout, wind, snow crunch

locations.description: A rural Russian peasant homestead — frozen threshing floor, birch brooms, oat sheaves, lamplight on family faces. The domestic counterpoint to the Caucasus front: here the cost of empire is measured in a wife's grief, a father's silence, and a letter read aloud at the town inn.

production_design.physical_signature: Frozen threshing floor under pre-dawn moonlight, steam of breath
production_design.color_palette: Blue-white frost, straw yellow, lamplight orange
production_design.soundscape: Creak of frozen wood, low voices, distant village dogs

locations.description: The residence of Commander-in-Chief Michael Vorontsov in Tiflis — wide portico, liveried footmen, thirty-cover dinner table, silver tureens. The seat of Russian imperial power in the Caucasus, where Hadji Murad is simultaneously honoured guest and useful political object, paraded before Tiflis society.

production_design.physical_signature: Long dinner table in candlelight, footmen in knee-breeches, silver service on white linen
production_design.color_palette: Imperial gold, white linen, deep crimson upholstery, chandelier sparkle
production_design.soundscape: Crystal, low dinner conversation, distant Oriental music, formal silence

locations.description: The newly built Tiflis theatre decorated in Oriental style, and the Viceregal Palace ballroom with its hidden orchestra. Together they form the social stage where Russian colonial society performs civilisation — and where Hadji Murad is exhibited as a trophy-guest.

production_design.physical_signature: Oriental tile work and gas-lit stage; ball-room with band hidden behind potted trees
production_design.color_palette: Gilt and ivory, Oriental turquoise accents, dancing candlelight
key_objects: HM's silver-hilted dagger at his belt
production_design.soundscape: Italian opera aria, waltz, murmur of appraisal, HM's silence

locations.canonical_name: HM's Remembered Mountain Origins (Tselmess / Khunzakh / Khansha's Palace)
locations.description: The landscape of Hadji Murad's backstory — the tiny birth-aoul of Tselmess, the Avar Khans' seat at Khunzakh, the Khansha's palace where he grew up alongside the young Khans. Rendered in memory sequences: warm, privileged, already tinged with the violence to come.

production_design.physical_signature: Stone palace on a high ridge, two cannon-shots from a cluster of mountain dwellings
production_design.color_palette: Warm sandstone gold, mountain blue sky, memory-hazed amber
production_design.atmosphere: Lost world — loyalty, privilege, and fatal entanglement
production_design.soundscape: Mountain wind, children's voices, distant prayer call

locations.description: The military encampment of Hamzad bek, approached through a mile-long murid escort with singing and rifle fire — the theatrically festive approach that masks the massacre of the Avar Khans. This location crystallises the betrayal that set Hadji Murad's entire arc in motion.

production_design.physical_signature: Open mountain encampment, celebratory riders caracoling, then sudden stillness before bloodshed
production_design.color_palette: Mountain bronze, gunpowder grey, blood crimson
production_design.soundscape: Singing, rifle salutes, then screams, then silence

locations.canonical_name: Mansokha Path — The Legendary Escape (Mountain Abyss)
locations.description: A narrow mountain path beside a 120-yard abyss — the site of Hadji Murad's legendary leap to escape Russian captivity. Rendered as myth within the film: other characters speak of it before we see it. The physical embodiment of his reputation for the impossible.

production_design.physical_signature: Path barely wide enough for a horse, sheer rock drop into void below
production_design.color_palette: White limestone, abyss shadow, haze of distance
production_design.atmosphere: Vertiginous myth — beauty and annihilation in the same frame
key_objects: Russian escort falling back in shock
production_design.soundscape: Wind, horse's hooves scrabbling, silence of the abyss

locations.canonical_name: Winter Palace — St. Petersburg (Nicholas I's Court)
locations.description: The rebuilt Winter Palace in St. Petersburg — lofty cabinet with four large windows and Alexander I's portrait, carpeted staircases, lackeys in gala livery, the Pompeian Hall for state dinners. Nicholas I is dissected here in all his autocratic theatricality: the spartan bedroom, the morning embankment walk, the masquerade assignations. The polar opposite of the Caucasus mountains.

production_design.physical_signature: Enormous writing table beneath portrait of Alexander I; Nicholas's camp-style narrow bed against empire decor
production_design.color_palette: Imperial white-and-gold, cold St. Petersburg grey at windows, Pompeian red at state dinner
production_design.atmosphere: Absolute power performing itself — theatrical austerity masking corruption
production_design.soundscape: Silence of absolute authority, shuffling courtiers, distant Neva wind

locations.description: Shamil's seat of power — a large mountain aoul whose entire population lines the rooftops and streets for the Imam's return. Inner and outer courtyards, a council chamber, a pit-prison seven feet deep, Ibrahim Raschid's saklya where Hadji Murad's family is held. The Chechen mirror-image of imperial Petersburg: authority enforced by religious terror rather than secular pageant.

production_design.physical_signature: Crowd on rooftops firing rifles; behind them, a pit-prison where Yusuf hears but cannot see
production_design.color_palette: Dust-gold, deep shadow, the grey of confinement
production_design.atmosphere: Theocratic authority — celebration and imprisonment simultaneous
production_design.soundscape: Rifle salutes, chanting, Yusuf's muffled voice from the pit, Patimat's silence

locations.description: A Russian frontier fort where Hadji Murad is billeted alongside Butler, sharing adjacent rooms. The cross-cultural friendship between HM and Butler develops in this spartan military space — a zone of genuine human contact across imperial division.

production_design.physical_signature: Two adjacent rooms in a frontier fort billet, shared wall, minimal furnishings
production_design.color_palette: Grey timber, steel, brief colour from HM's Chechen weapons and dress
production_design.atmosphere: Cautious male friendship across unbridgeable difference
production_design.soundscape: Fort routine sounds, low conversation, distant bugle

fused_from: Petrovsk Fort (Butler's quarters / Hadji Murad's room)

locations.description: A five-room house near the mosque and Khan's palace in the small Muslim town of Nukha — Hadji Murad's comfortable but surveilled residence. He is close enough to Islamic practice to feel at home, close enough to Russian supervision to feel imprisoned. His murids share the space; officers and an interpreter are always present.

production_design.physical_signature: Five-room house with mosque visible from window — comfort and surveillance in the same view
production_design.color_palette: Warm terracotta, afternoon sunlight, shadow of bars implied but not shown
production_design.atmosphere: Gilded confinement — generous hospitality as control
production_design.soundscape: Call to prayer, Russian officer's boots on tile, HM's deliberate silences

locations.canonical_name: Road Beyond Nukha — The Final Ride
locations.description: The rural road past the Nukha fort perimeter — buffaloes, wagons, women with baskets — where Hadji Murad makes his final break. He turns left toward the river and the flooded rice-fields rather than toward the expected mountain direction, a deceptive route that briefly looks like freedom.

production_design.physical_signature: Open road with working people, then the sudden left turn toward river — ordinary life punctuated by fugitive urgency
production_design.color_palette: Dusty road yellow, spring green water-meadow, bright sky
production_design.atmosphere: False calm before the final trap
production_design.soundscape: Hoofbeats accelerating, shouts of pursuit, water rushing ahead

locations.description: Boggy, flooded rice paddies between Hadji Murad's escape route and the Alazan River — impassable in spring flood. This is where his evasion route collapses and the pursuit closes in, trapping him in terrain that looked open and proved fatal.

production_design.physical_signature: Ankle-deep flooded paddy fields, horses struggling in mud, horizon-wide and inescapable
production_design.color_palette: Reed silver, muddy ochre, sky-reflected water, no cover
production_design.soundscape: Splashing hooves, wind, crows, distant pursuit shouts

locations.canonical_name: Thicket Ditch — HM's Final Defensive Position
locations.description: A raised patch of ground overgrown with shrubs, containing an old ditch in a rice-field bog — Hadji Murad's last stand. He and his murids fortify it with cut branches and earth, pray, and fight until they are overrun. The thistle's closing counterpart: beautiful, solitary, destroyed.

production_design.physical_signature: Low earthen-and-branch parapet in a bog thicket, entirely surrounded — a fortress the size of a garden
production_design.color_palette: Brown earth, grey-green scrub, blood red, final white sky
production_design.atmosphere: Tragic sublime — resistance without hope, beauty in annihilation
production_design.soundscape: Rifle volley, HM's final shout, then silence, then wind

locations_raw.name: Midsummer Fields
locations_raw.description: Lush agricultural landscape with hay-harvested and rye-ripening fields, wildflowers in full bloom, a ditch with a crimson thistle, and a large ploughed black-earth field belonging to a landed proprietor

locations_raw.name: Ploughed Black-Earth Field
locations_raw.description: Large, evenly furrowed, moist black-earth field belonging to a landed proprietor. Lifeless, no grass or plant — except the solitary battered thistle the narrator encounters again.

locations_raw.name: Makhmet Aoul
locations_raw.description: Hostile Chechen village fifteen miles from Russian territory; filled with kizyak smoke, crowded saklyas like honeycomb cells, a fountain below — sounds of cattle, sheep, disputing men, women and children.

locations_raw.name: Chechen Aoul (Arrival Village)
locations_raw.description: A hillside Chechen village with narrow side streets; saklytas built into the hillside with rooftop sleeping areas, wooden-heeled slippers, clay chimneys; intimate domestic space contrasting with Hadji Murad's former martial grandeur.

locations_raw.name: Sado's Saklya
locations_raw.description: Interior of a Chechen mountain dwelling — clay-plastered, whitewashed walls; weapons hung on nails between gleaming basins; cushions along the front wall; earthen floor. Domestic, intimate, martial.

locations_raw.name: Mitchit Aoul
locations_raw.description: Nearby Chechen village mentioned as having had its hay burned by Russian troops — offscreen but dramatically present as evidence of imperial violence.

locations_raw.name: Sado's Saklya (Aoul interior)
locations_raw.description: Interior of Sado's Chechen house; felt mat floor, wooden door with audible steps outside; ritual space of kunak hospitality and conspiratorial planning.

locations_raw.name: Argun River bend
locations_raw.description: Where the river Argun bends by a cliff; a forest glade nearby holds two haystacks where Hadji Murad's four murids wait; remote meeting point for fugitives.

locations_raw.name: Sado's Saklya (Interior)
locations_raw.description: Interior of Sado's Chechen mountain house: low round table, cushioned floor seating; domestic intimacy contrasting with the danger outside; warm, lamp-lit, private

locations_raw.name: Vozvizhensk Fort
locations_raw.description: Russian advanced fort approximately ten miles from Sado's aoul; sends nightly ambush patrols beyond the Shahgirinsk Gate into the surrounding forest.

locations_raw.name: Shahgirinsk Gate
locations_raw.description: Gate of Vozvizhensk Fort beyond which Russian patrols venture into the forested darkness; the boundary between fort-safety and Chechen wilderness.

locations_raw.name: Ambush Plane Tree
locations_raw.description: Blackened trunk of a broken plane tree ~520 paces from the fort gate; the standard Russian night-ambush position in the surrounding forest.

locations_raw.name: Night Ambush Outpost (near Fort)
locations_raw.description: Forest edge outpost, plane trees, night sky; guards positioned to prevent mountaineers from moving a cannon unobserved toward the Russian fort

locations_raw.name: Caucasus Night Encampment (Plane Tree Grove)
locations_raw.description: A forested outpost position where Russian soldiers keep night watch; plane trees, wind, stars, and the howling of jackals define the environment.

locations_raw.name: Vozdvizhensk Fort Interior (Vorontsov's House)
locations_raw.description: Best house in the fort, still lit while barracks are dark; home of Simon Vorontsov, the regimental commander

locations_raw.name: Vorontsov's House (Fort Interior)
locations_raw.description: Spacious drawing room with carpeted floor, rich curtains, four-candle card table; the most luxurious dwelling in the Vozdvizhensk Fort; midnight domestic theatre of imperial privilege

locations_raw.name: Poltoratsky's Quarters
locations_raw.description: A small shared house near the fort; spartan billet for Poltoratsky and Tikhonov

locations_raw.name: Ravine Outside Aoul
locations_raw.description: A forested ravine with rapidly running water at the bottom, crossed by Hadji Murad during his nighttime escape from the aoul; marks the boundary between the village world and the wilderness

locations_raw.name: Forest Glade Camp
locations_raw.description: A hidden clearing in the dark forest where Hadji Murad's murids wait around a bonfire; horses hobbled and saddled, camp quickly extinguished on Hadji Murad's order; transitional refuge between Chechen territory and Russian lines

locations_raw.name: Shagirinsk Gate
locations_raw.description: Russian military checkpoint / fortification gate six miles from the Shalin glade woodcutting site; Poltoratsky's companies march out from here before dawn

locations_raw.name: Forest Outpost Ravine
locations_raw.description: Skirts of a forest above a bare descending ravine slope; Russian outpost position where shots are exchanged with Chechen forces

locations_raw.name: Ravine Outpost / Forest Skirt (near Vozdvizhensk)
locations_raw.description: Forested ravine edge where Russian outposts hold a line; open descending slope visible across the ravine. Scene of Avdeev's wounding. Contiguous with the Shalin Glade operational zone.

locations_raw.name: Vorontsov Drawing Room (Vozdvizhensk)
locations_raw.description: The domestic interior of the fort commander's house; site of the first informal Russian-Hadji Murad social encounter; armchairs, coffee service, child at play

locations_raw.name: Meller-Zakomelsky's Residence (Waiting Room)
locations_raw.description: The general's house in Vozdvizhensk or nearby garrison; the waiting room where Hadji Murad is held signals subordination and bureaucratic power over him.

locations_raw.name: Fort Hospital Ward
locations_raw.description: Small wooden building roofed with boards at the entrance of Vozdvizhensk Fort; common ward with four beds, currently holding four patients before Avdeev's arrival

locations_raw.name: Avdeev Family Farm
locations_raw.description: Rural Russian peasant homestead; frozen threshing floor under moonlight; birch brooms, oat sheaves; hard winter morning

locations_raw.name: Avdeev Family Farm (Threshing Floor)
locations_raw.description: Rural peasant homestead threshing floor, pre-dawn winter setting, frozen snow underfoot; domestic counterpart to the Caucasus front where Avdeev is dying.

locations_raw.name: Avdeev Family Home (village interior)
locations_raw.description: Rural Russian peasant household; well-to-do family with hidden savings; domestic space of grief and domestic conflict over absent conscript

locations_raw.name: Avdeev Family Farmstead (Town-Market Visit)
locations_raw.description: Small Russian peasant village setting; old man drives loaded sledges to town inn where the letter is read aloud.

locations_raw.name: Tiflis Viceregal Palace
locations_raw.description: Michael Vorontsov's Tiflis palace: wide porch, sentinel, drawing room with thirty dinner guests. Seat of Russian imperial power in the Caucasus.

locations_raw.name: Tiflis Viceregal Dining Room
locations_raw.description: Grand dining room in Vorontsov's palace; long table with thirty guests, footmen in livery and knee-breeches, silver tureen — theatre of imperial social ritual.

locations_raw.name: Temir Khan Shura
locations_raw.description: Town raided by Hadji Murad in 1849 in broad daylight; shops plundered; referenced as part of his growing legend at the Tiflis dinner.

locations_raw.name: Vorontsov's Waiting Room (Tiflis Palace)
locations_raw.description: Antechamber of the Tiflis Viceregal Palace where petitioners of all kinds queue: generals, merchants, widows, Georgian nobles, officials, and now Hadji Murad.

locations_raw.name: Tiflis New Theater
locations_raw.description: Newly built theater decorated in Oriental style; site of Italian opera performance; Hadji Murad attends with Loris-Melikov and is observed by the Tiflis social elite

locations_raw.name: Vorontsov Palace Ballroom
locations_raw.description: Large, brightly lit reception hall in the Viceregal Palace; band hidden among trees; site of the Monday evening party where Hadji Murad encounters Russian high society for the first time

locations_raw.name: Tselmess
locations_raw.description: Hadji Murad's birthplace — small aoul 'the size of an ass's head,' two cannon-shots from Khunzakh.

locations_raw.name: Khunzakh
locations_raw.description: Seat of the Avar Khans; HM's family was closely connected through wet-nursing relationships.

locations_raw.name: Khansha's Palace
locations_raw.description: The palace where Hadji Murad grew up alongside the three young Khans; site of privilege, loyalty, and the world he lost. Part of his backstory memory, not a present-day location.

locations_raw.name: Grandfather's Farmstead
locations_raw.description: Remote farmstead of Hadji Murad's maternal grandfather, a silversmith. Evoked in childhood memory; signals clan roots and craft tradition.

locations_raw.name: Tiflis (Russian Command HQ)
locations_raw.description: Site of the Russian Commander-in-Chief Baron Rosen; where Umma Khan is corrupted by Russian officers

locations_raw.name: Hamzad's Camp (near Khunzakh)
locations_raw.description: Hamzad's military encampment approached via a mile-long murid escort with singing, shooting, and caracoling; site of the deceptive reception of the Avar Khans and the subsequent massacre.

locations_raw.name: Khunzakh (Khansha's stronghold)
locations_raw.description: The seat of the Avar Khanate; where Hadji Murad warns the Khansha not to send her son — his warning is ignored.

locations_raw.name: Mansokha Path
locations_raw.description: Narrow mountain path beside a 120-yard abyss; site of Hadji Murad's legendary escape from Russian custody

locations_raw.name: Temir-Khan-Shura
locations_raw.description: Destination to which the Russian soldiers were escorting the captured Hadji Murad

locations_raw.name: Avaria
locations_raw.description: Region Hadji Murad was appointed to govern by General Rosen under Russian authority

locations_raw.name: Mansokha Path (abyss)
locations_raw.description: Narrow mountain path above a ~120-yard-deep ravine; site of Hadji Murad's legendary survival leap — already in location graph as top cinematic anchor.

locations_raw.name: Tabasaran
locations_raw.description: Region where Shamil sent Hadji Murad on a punitive/test mission; site of his last successful raid before the break with Shamil

locations_raw.name: Shoushin District
locations_raw.description: Administrative district under Tarkhanov's command; mentioned as context for his presence

locations_raw.name: St. Petersburg (implied courier destination)
locations_raw.description: Imperial capital receiving Vorontsov's Caucasian report; seat of Chernyshov and Nicholas I.

locations_raw.name: Winter Palace Entrance
locations_raw.description: Grand entrance to the St. Petersburg Winter Palace; cold January morning, sledges, coachmen waiting outside in frozen air

locations_raw.name: Winter Palace Waiting-Room and Staircase
locations_raw.description: Carpeted steps, Court lackeys in gala livery, antechamber outside Nicholas's cabinet — the antechamber of absolute imperial power, rendered as theatre of obsequiousness

locations_raw.name: Nicholas's Cabinet (Winter Palace)
locations_raw.description: Lofty reception room in the upper story of the rebuilt Winter Palace — four large windows, portrait of Alexander I, enormous writing table, arm chair for Nicholas, chairs along walls for officials

locations_raw.name: Masquerade Box
locations_raw.description: A private velvet-furnished box at the masquerade venue, kept reserved for Nicholas's assignations

locations_raw.name: Nicholas's Rendezvous Room
locations_raw.description: The private space where Nicholas habitually conducts his sexual encounters

locations_raw.name: Nicholas's Bedroom
locations_raw.description: Spartan room with a hard narrow bed; Nicholas sleeps under a cloak he likens to Napoleon's hat — performative austerity

locations_raw.name: Palace Embankment (St. Petersburg)
locations_raw.description: The waterfront embankment outside the Winter Palace where Nicholas walks each morning in military cloak and cap; site of impromptu encounters with subjects.

locations_raw.name: Winter Palace Small Portico
locations_raw.description: The private exit/entrance Nicholas uses for his morning walk; through it he re-enters from the embankment and observes arriving carriages at the Saltykov entrance.

locations_raw.name: Saltykov Entrance (Winter Palace)
locations_raw.description: A specific entrance to the Winter Palace where Helena Pavlovna's carriage is seen arriving; exterior, briefly glimpsed

locations_raw.name: Nicholas's Cabinet (Report Room)
locations_raw.description: The private cabinet within the imperial apartments where Nicholas receives morning reports from ministers; intimate seat of autocratic power

locations_raw.name: Pompeian Hall (Winter Palace)
locations_raw.description: Imperial dining hall in the Winter Palace where the New Year dinner is hosted; lavish, classicizing décor underscores the theatrical self-presentation of the Romanov court.

locations_raw.name: Pompeian Hall
locations_raw.description: Imperial dining hall where Nicholas hosts state dinner with foreign dignitaries

locations_raw.name: Chechnya March Route
locations_raw.description: Winter Caucasus landscape — stream crossing, tilled fields, forest-clad hills, snowy peaks; the column's path into hostile territory for the January 1852 raid.

locations_raw.name: Unnamed Chechen Aoul (Burned)
locations_raw.description: A Chechen village entered at a run after Cossack pursuit; found deserted; corn, hay, and saklyas ordered burned by Russian soldiers

locations_raw.name: Maize Field Ravine
locations_raw.description: Stubbly maize field crossing off the main road leading toward the forest where the skirmish unfolds; second ravine reveals the aoul

locations_raw.name: Deserted Chechen Aoul (raid site)
locations_raw.description: An unnamed mountain-side aoul evacuated before the Russian column arrived; burned during the raid. Fusable with other destroyed aoul locations (Sado's Aoul remains the named anchor).

locations_raw.name: Vozdvizhensk Fort (return approach)
locations_raw.description: March route back to the fort after patrol; night air, snow hills visible ~100 miles away

locations_raw.name: Sado's Aoul (destroyed)
locations_raw.description: The same Chechen village where Hadji Murad spent his first night before surrendering — now burnt ruins, desecrated mosque and fountain, dead son brought to mosque on horseback

locations_raw.name: Vedeno
locations_raw.description: Shamil's stronghold aoul where Hadji Murad's family is imprisoned; large settlement with public celebrations on Shamil's return

locations_raw.name: Ibrahim Raschid's Saklya
locations_raw.description: House in Vedeno where Hadji Murad's women and children are kept under guard

locations_raw.name: Vedeno Pit-Prison
locations_raw.description: A pit more than seven feet deep used as a prison; Yusuf held here with seven other criminals

locations_raw.name: Shamil's Aoul (Vedeno approach)
locations_raw.description: A large aoul whose entire population lines the streets and rooftops for the Imam's arrival; firing rifles and pistols in celebration.

locations_raw.name: HM Family Saklya (penthouse)
locations_raw.description: Interior of Hadji Murad's family dwelling where his wives watch from the penthouse; Patimat remains inside by the dying hearth.

locations_raw.name: Prison Pit
locations_raw.description: A dark, fetid underground pit where Yusuf and other prisoners are held — they can hear but not see Shamil's triumphal procession.

locations_raw.name: Shamil's Outer Court (Vedeno)
locations_raw.description: Large courtyard at the gates of Shamil's compound, crowded with petitioners, supplicants, and those summoned for judgment; guarded by two armed Lesghians

locations_raw.name: Shamil's Inner Court (Vedeno)
locations_raw.description: Private inner court separated from the outer by gates; penthouse apartment to the left of the gate; seraglio behind a fence dividing men's and women's quarters

locations_raw.name: Shamil's Guest Chamber (Vedeno)
locations_raw.description: Council room in Vedeno where Shamil's six elder advisors assemble; distinct from his private quarters; formal judicial-political space

locations_raw.name: Shamil's Council Chamber (Vedeno)
locations_raw.description: Interior court setting where Shamil sits on a larger cushion, hears criminal cases under Shariat law, and deliberates political strategy with his naib councilors.

locations_raw.name: Shamil's Wives' Quarters (Vedeno)
locations_raw.description: The domestic enclosure behind the fence at Vedeno where Shamil's wives live in separate rooms; site of private marital politics invisible to his public authority

locations_raw.name: Petrovsk Fort (Butler's quarters / Hadji Murad's room)
locations_raw.description: The Russian frontier fort where Hadji Murad is billeted; Butler's room and HM's adjacent room serve as meeting space for their cross-cultural friendship

locations_raw.name: Russian Field Camp (left flank)
locations_raw.description: Military encampment with splendid cloth tents; Vorontsov's command tent; long table set for formal dinner; mountaineers occasionally shell it

locations_raw.name: Nukha
locations_raw.description: Small Muslim town in Transcaucasia; Hadji Murad requests relocation here for proximity to Shamil negotiations and Islamic prayer. Becomes his gilded cage.

locations_raw.name: Nukha House (Hadji Murad's quarters)
locations_raw.description: Five-room house near the mosque and Khan's palace in Nukha; shared with officers, interpreter, and henchmen; serves as Hadji Murad's gilded-cage residence

locations_raw.name: Vozdvizhensk Fort (officers' mess / barracks)
locations_raw.description: Interior of the fort during a military dinner; soldiers quartered in barracks, officers sharing quarters with the Kabarda regiment newcomers.

locations_raw.name: Road beyond Nukha fort
locations_raw.description: Rural road past the fort perimeter; buffaloes, wagons, women with baskets; open country with river visible to the left; site of HM's final ride

locations_raw.name: Benerdzhik Aoul
locations_raw.description: Aoul visible to the right of the road with saklyas and minarets, near the point where HM turns left toward the river.

locations_raw.name: Al-Azani River approach
locations_raw.description: Fields and river to the left of the road; HM's chosen evasion route away from the expected mountain direction.

locations_raw.name: Alazan River Flooded Rice-Fields
locations_raw.description: Boggy, flooded rice paddies between HM's escape route and the Alazan River; impassable in spring flood

locations_raw.name: Shrub Thicket / Night Shelter
locations_raw.description: Patch of higher ground overgrown with shrubs where HM and murids rest horses and pray before pursuers arrive

locations_raw.name: Thicket Ditch (Final Entrenchment)
locations_raw.description: A rice-field bog thicket with an old ditch; Hadji Murad's final defensive position, fortified with cut branches and earth

timeline.narrative_beat: Thistle establishes the film's moral question: what is heroic resistance?

timeline.period_label: November 1851: Hadji Murad's Flight from Shamil
timeline.narrative_beat: HM shelters with Sado, makes his decision to surrender to the Russians

timeline.period_label: Late November 1851: HM Crosses the Russian Line
timeline.narrative_beat: HM surrenders; Poltoratsky escorts him; first contact with Russian authority

timeline.period_label: November–December 1851: The Skirmish and Avdeev's Wounding
timeline.narrative_beat: Avdeev shot — the first casualty that personalises the empire's cost

timeline.period_label: December 1851: Avdeev's Death at the Fort
timeline.narrative_beat: Avdeev dies in hospital; his death letter reaches his family
Family receives death news — empire's human cost registered on home front
HM presented to Vorontsov and Tiflis society; negotiation and spectacle

timeline.period_label: January 1852: HM as Social Trophy in Tiflis
timeline.narrative_beat: HM at opera and Monday ball — colonial society's fascination and condescension

timeline.primary_location: HM's Remembered Mountain Origins (Tselmess / Khunzakh / Khansha's Palace)
timeline.narrative_beat: HM's childhood, rise, and fatal loyalties reconstructed

timeline.period_label: Memory: Hamzad's Betrayal of the Avar Khans
timeline.narrative_beat: Root event: HM witnesses the massacre that began his war

timeline.period_label: Memory: Mansokha Escape — HM's Legend Established
timeline.primary_location: Mansokha Path — The Legendary Escape (Mountain Abyss)
timeline.narrative_beat: HM's impossible survival leap — the myth that precedes him everywhere

timeline.period_label: January 1852 (parallel): Nicholas I in St. Petersburg
timeline.primary_location: Winter Palace — St. Petersburg (Nicholas I's Court)
timeline.narrative_beat: Nicholas receives Caucasus report; autocracy, masquerade, moral bankruptcy dissected

timeline.period_label: January–February 1852: Shamil and the Hostage Family
timeline.narrative_beat: Shamil holds HM's family; Yusuf in pit; theocratic mirror of imperial cruelty

timeline.period_label: February 1852: HM with Butler at Petrovsk
timeline.narrative_beat: HM and Butler's friendship — the one genuine human crossing in the film

timeline.period_label: March–April 1852: HM Under Supervision in Nukha
timeline.narrative_beat: HM's captivity tightens; negotiations stall; he resolves to escape

timeline.primary_location: Road Beyond Nukha — The Final Ride
timeline.narrative_beat: HM breaks from his escort and rides toward the river
Escape route blocked by flood; HM's choices collapse

timeline.period_label: April 1852: HM's Last Stand and Death
timeline.primary_location: Thicket Ditch — HM's Final Defensive Position
timeline.narrative_beat: HM and murids make final stand; HM killed and beheaded — the thistle destroyed

production_notes.macro_visual_strategy: The film operates on two irreconcilable visual registers: MOUNTAIN (raw, vertical, textured — Caucasus ridges, low saklya ceilings, firelight, mud, stone, cold air) versus PALACE (horizontal, gilded, upholstered — Winter Palace loft, Tiflis dinner table, chandelier light, white linen). Every cut between these worlds is a moral argument. Hadji Murad moves through both and belongs to neither. The thistle of the prologue — singular, crimson, crushed but still upright — is the visual thesis that both worlds destroy without understanding.
key_visual_motifs: The crimson thistle (prologue and epilogue mirror)
Firelight vs. chandelier light (mountain warmth vs. imperial cold luxury)
Vertical stone drops — the Mansokha abyss, the Vedeno prison pit, the ravine outposts
Snow and blood in the same frame (Avdeev's wounding, HM's final stand)
The open door or gate as threshold between worlds (Shahgirinsk Gate, Tiflis anteroom, Nukha window with minaret)
geographic_anchors: Caucasus Greater Mountain Range (Chechen/Avar highland aoul world — approx. 1500-2500m elevation)
Tiflis / Tbilisi (Vorontsov's Caucasus imperial capital — transition zone between worlds)
St. Petersburg / Winter Palace (absolute imperial centre — the source of all orders)
Nukha / Sheki, Azerbaijan (HM's gilded-cage liminal zone — neither mountain nor court)
production_notes.color_strategy: The mountain world begins in saturated earth tones — ochre, terracotta, firelight amber — and progressively desaturates as Hadji Murad moves deeper into Russian control. The imperial world (Tiflis, Petersburg) is always golden but cold: gilded surfaces that produce no warmth. The final sequence at the thicket ditch returns the palette to raw earth, with the single red of HM's blood echoing the opening thistle — suggesting not defeat but completion of an unbroken moral arc. Nicholas I's Petersburg sequences use a slightly blue-shifted, bleached palette to underscore their moral sterility despite their material splendour.
production_notes.compression_rationale: The 89 raw locations extracted from Tolstoi's novel reflect a panoramic, multi-POV literary structure unsuitable for a 120-minute feature. The consolidated 19 canonical locations serve the film's three-act spine: Act I (HM's flight and surrender — mountain and frontier fort), Act II (negotiation, surveillance, and parallel worlds — Tiflis, Petersburg, Vedeno, Petrovsk, Nukha), Act III (escape attempt and death — road, floodplain, thicket). Dropped locations are either (a) duplicate descriptors of the same physical space, (b) backstory references with no shootable scene, or (c) transitional travel descriptions that serve the novel's pace but not cinema's economy. The compression preserves every major narrative beat while reducing location count by 79%, making the production physically feasible as a single-camera historical feature.

recurring_locations: Caucasus Wilderness — Forest, Ravine & Escape Routes
Mansokha Path — The Legendary Escape (Mountain Abyss)
