refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as Hadji Murad learning later (via murid report or his own glance) that his pursuers witnessed his crossing — converts background detail into HM's awareness of being observed and hunted even in Russian territory. CF-08 / NF-04.

refocalize.scene: Poltoratsky riding up and ordering fire without tactical necessity
refocalize.recommended_pov: Can stay as-is — Poltoratsky's POV here is not load-bearing for HM's arc, but the Avdeev consequence IS. Keep Avdeev-centric framing; Poltoratsky reduced to functional commander role, not POV anchor.

refocalize.scene: Vorontsov rides to meet HM / announces surrender to Poltoratsky
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage from Hadji Murad's POV — he approaches the Russian line; we see the fort, the sharpshooters, Poltoratsky's incomprehension. His smile is the scene's emotional anchor and it plays stronger as his conscious choice than as something Poltoratsky witnesses. CF-08 / NF-09

refocalize.scene: Avdeev refuses vodka / is carried to the fort
refocalize.recommended_pov: Can remain off-HM; but in film compress to a single cutaway before HM's arrival — SC-02 (dual-function: establishes mortality + clears stage for HM entrance)

refocalize.scene: Poltoratsky's surprised observation of HM's childlike smile and demeanor
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage through HM's eyes — what does HM read in the Russian officer's face? The refocalization preserves the surprise-of-reputation beat while anchoring POV on protagonist.

refocalize.scene: Vorontsov's interior anxiety about Meller-Zakomelsky jurisdictional breach
refocalize.recommended_pov: CUT or compress to single beat visible through HM's eyes — HM notices Vorontsov's smile falter, reads the politics without understanding the bureaucracy; CF-08, NF-04

refocalize.scene: Marya Vasilevna's drawing-room impression of Hadji Murad (her POV: his blush, his smile, her inclination toward him)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage through Hadji Murad's eyes: the strange pale world of Russian domesticity, the child, the unfamiliar rituals — same scene, protagonist as witness, no loss of dramatic content

refocalize.scene: Vorontsov retreats to his office to write reports — narrator follows him, leaving HM alone
refocalize.recommended_pov: Cut the office scene; convey report-writing via a brief line of dialogue or visual — stay with Hadji Murad in the drawing room for continuity of protagonist POV (CF-08)

refocalize.scene: Vorontsov-Marya Vasilevna French dialogue about the dagger
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator reporting both sides of French exchange
refocalize.recommended_pov: Stay on Hadji Murad's face — he cannot understand French; film shows exclusion through HM's uncomprehending stillness, not through translated subtitles. → CF-08, NF-04

refocalize.scene: Hadji Murad's private room — checks on murids via Eldar
refocalize.recommended_pov: Keep in HM's POV — fears shown through behavior (checking dagger, sitting armed) not narrated. Tolstoi's explicit 'he feared everything: that he might be seized...' becomes visual action. → NF-04, NF-09

refocalize.scene: Meller-Zakomelsky separates Hadji Murad and sends him to the waiting room
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage from Hadji Murad's POV — he is ushered away, left alone, hears Russian voices behind a closed door. His isolation and the reversal from honoured guest to detained subject land harder when experienced through his eyes. CF-08 / NF-09.

refocalize.scene: Marya Vasilevna's observation about the poisoning fear at dinner
refocalize.recommended_pov: Keep beat but anchor it in HM's observable action (choosing his portion) — camera records the act, Vorontsov and Marya react. Removes need for her explanatory line; visual subtext suffices. NF-04 (cut authorial comment), SC-06 (translate inner fear to visual).

refocalize.scene: Jurisdictional quarrel in Meller-Zakomelsky's study
refocalize.current_pov: Third-person omniscient alternating between Vorontsov and Meller-Zakomelsky
refocalize.recommended_pov: Hadji Murad waiting in the anteroom — intercut with muffled argument heard through the door; his stillness and powerlessness foregrounded — CF-08, NF-04

refocalize.scene: Marya Vasilevna's laughing intervention
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator; Marya Vasilevna as active agent
refocalize.recommended_pov: Hadji Murad's POV — aide-de-camp's expression shift as laughter leaks from the study; HM reads the room without understanding the language — CF-08

refocalize.scene: Vorontsov-Meller-Zakomelsky reconciliation and decision
refocalize.recommended_pov: HM's POV — he sits in the next room reading the muffled sounds and body language through a doorway or thin wall; the scene becomes his silent intelligence-gathering moment rather than the officers' domestic resolution. CF-08, NF-09.

refocalize.scene: Avdeev in the hospital ward
refocalize.recommended_pov: Keep as Avdeev's own POV (he is the de facto protagonist of this strand). The ward patients, the doctor's examination, the scars — all filtered through Avdeev's pain-narrowed consciousness. NF-09 applied to secondary protagonist strand.

refocalize.scene: Avdeev's death scene — entire chunk
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator / Avdeev (Tolstoi stays close to Avdeev throughout)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Keep as Avdeev POV — this is one of the few scenes where camera SHOULD leave Hadji Murad. The parallel death (HM surrounded by enemies, Avdeev surrounded by comrades) gains power through intercutting. Recommend: intercut Avdeev death scene with HM's captivity/isolation in Tiflis as structural contrast, not refocalize away from it.
refocalize.deviation_note: CF-08 recommends refocal onto HM, but M-02 (thematic priority) overrides: Avdeev's death IS the anti-imperial theme made flesh. Cutting or refocalizing it collapses the film's moral argument. KEEP as parallel POV, use for intercutting.

refocalize.scene: Avdeev family farm threshing floor — Chapter VIII
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator observing Russian peasant home simultaneously with soldier's death
refocalize.recommended_pov: Keep as cross-cut counterpoint; optionally frame as what Avdeev imagines / hallucinates in his final moment — NF-09, CF-08

refocalize.scene: Official military dispatch read by narrator
refocalize.recommended_pov: Translate to visual: show dispatch being written/stamped by a clerk while Avdeev's body is being wrapped — NF-04, SC-06

refocalize.scene: Entire Avdeev family threshing-floor scene
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator following Russian peasant family at home
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as Avdeev's dying memory/hallucination (intercut with death-bed scene in Caucasus) so HM's story-world remains primary frame; OR condense to a single cutaway — letter arriving at home — reported to HM-world via Butler's observation. CF-08, NF-04.

refocalize.scene: Avdeev family quarrel and ruble dispatch
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator (Avdeev family, no HM presence)
refocalize.recommended_pov: REFOCAL — cut to Peter/Avdeev receiving the letter at the fort, restaging the family grief from his end; or DISSOLVE entirely and convey via Avdeev's deathbed letter-reading in earlier chunk

refocalize.scene: Entire Avdeev family home-front mourning sequence (letter sent, returned, requiem)
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator following the Avdeev peasant family in rural Russia
refocalize.recommended_pov: Reframe as consequence seen from the fort or compressed into a single scene: a comrade (Panov or Serogin) learning of the returned letter, collapsing the home-front POV into a soldier's reaction. Alternatively, cut to a visual insert — the returned envelope, a candle lit — with no dialogue. CF-08 / NF-04.

refocalize.scene: Aksinya's grief and inner secret joy
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator (Tolstoi irony — shows public vs. private Aksinya)
refocalize.recommended_pov: CUT entirely — Hadji Murad has no access to this scene, it is wholly outside his trajectory. If Avdeev's fate must register, compress into a single environmental beat (empty bunk, comrades' silence). CF-08, NF-04.

refocalize.scene: Michael Vorontsov receiving the courier and entering the dinner party
refocalize.recommended_pov: KEEP but refocalize — Vorontsov IS a protagonist-adjacent antagonist (historical anchor). Scene can remain from his POV as the film needs to establish the imperial receiving-end of HM's surrender. However, the biographical backstory paragraph should be cut (NF-04) and replaced with behavioral cues in the scene itself. CF-08 (non-HM POV acceptable because Vorontsov is established antagonist, not a random side-character).

refocalize.scene: Dinner-table anecdote about Hadji Murad at Pahlen's detachment and the Dargo 'rescue'
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator explaining Vorontsov's Dargo history and the room's collective awareness of the gaffe
refocalize.recommended_pov: Stay on Vorontsov's face — his internal reaction (pride to wounded shame) is visible; cut the authorial exposition entirely. Manana's intervention lands harder if we don't over-explain. [NF-04, CF-08]

refocalize.scene: Entire Tiflis dinner conversation about Hadji Murad
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator observing courtiers and Vorontsov
refocalize.recommended_pov: REFOCAL via CF-08: restage as scene HM later learns about (he is informed that the Russians have been praising him at dinner — this arrives as a report from a murid or spy, sharpening his awareness that he is being instrumentalized as a political symbol rather than treated as an ally)

refocalize.scene: General's interrupted story
refocalize.current_pov: Authorial omniscient — we see the general's embarrassment and the aides-de-camp's warning looks
refocalize.recommended_pov: COMPRESS into Marya Vasilevna's POV: her social maneuvering becomes the visible action, cutting the general's interiority entirely — NF-04 (authorial narrator dissolved into character behavior)

refocalize.scene: Entire Tiflis dinner myth-making sequence
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as news that reaches Hadji Murad second-hand (a murid reports what the Russians say about him); or compress to a single expository beat in Hadji Murad's next scene → CF-08, NF-04

refocalize.scene: Simon's letter read aloud / jurisdictional quarrel summary
refocalize.recommended_pov: Cut entirely; Simon-Meller quarrel is cuttable thread (see plotline graph); its resolution has no bearing on HM's trajectory → SS-02, SC-01

refocalize.scene: Vorontsov reads Simon's letter and reacts with snuff-taking pleasure
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as scene HM is waiting and HEARS the aide-de-camp mention the letter from Simon — HM's awareness that his surrender is already being processed upstairs. Reframes bureaucratic comfort as HM's vulnerability. CF-08.

refocalize.scene: Waiting room tableau of petitioners
refocalize.recommended_pov: HM's POV entering the room — his eyes moving across the petitioners, reading the social hierarchy of supplication he has now joined. More filmable and keeps viewer inside HM's perspective. NF-09.

refocalize.scene: Vorontsov's silent internal reading of Hadji Murad (narrator reports both men's inner states simultaneously)
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator shuttles between Vorontsov's thoughts and Hadji Murad's perception
refocalize.recommended_pov: Stay on Hadji Murad's face/eyes reading Vorontsov — cut to Vorontsov's micro-expression; let audience decode, not narrator explain. CF-08, NF-04

refocalize.scene: Tolstoi's authorial aside revealing Vorontsov had 'long forgotten' the 1839 Avaria events despite saying 'I know, I know'
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator exposing Vorontsov's dissemblance directly
refocalize.recommended_pov: Translate to visual irony: Vorontsov's too-quick 'I know' + slight hesitation in his eyes, legible to the audience without narrator intervention — NF-04

refocalize.scene: Mutual silent truth-telling via eye contact (narrator's interior commentary on both men's unspoken understanding)
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator ventriloquizing both characters' interior states simultaneously
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as close-up reaction shot sequence — HM's gaze holds, Vorontsov's glance slides away first — conveying the power dynamic without any voice-over — NF-04, CF-08

refocalize.scene: Italian opera attendance and ballroom party at Vorontsovs'
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator observing Tiflis society from outside with ironic ethnographic distance
refocalize.recommended_pov: Stay on Hadji Murad's POV throughout — his impassivity as active resistance, not passive indifference; camera stays close to his face reading the alien spectacle; NF-04 (auktorial narrator dissolved into protagonist behavior) + CF-08 (refocalization on Tier-1 protagonist)

refocalize.scene: First Vorontsov interview ending — referral to chief of staff
refocalize.current_pov: Neutral narrator reports bureaucratic deflection flatly
refocalize.recommended_pov: HM's face/hands as Vorontsov speaks — register the non-answer landing; his family hostage declaration should play as the scene's emotional peak, not Vorontsov's vague promise; NF-09 applies

refocalize.scene: Ball as social spectacle — narrated from omniscient perspective observing all guests' behavior toward HM
refocalize.recommended_pov: HM's POV exclusively — camera stays close on his face and eyes; the half-dressed women and Vorontsov's epaulets read as alien objects he is cataloguing, not as 'normal' society. His attempt to speak about his family becomes the only moment of interiority. CF-08, NF-09

refocalize.scene: Loris-Melikov arrives to record HM's life story for the Tsar
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator sets up the institutional frame before HM speaks
refocalize.recommended_pov: Open on HM already seated, Loris-Melikov mid-explanation — cut the authorial setup, let the situation speak. NF-04

refocalize.scene: Loris-Melikov's framing of the dictation session (notebook, translation, sending to the Emperor)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Stay in Hadji Murad's POV — his flattered pause, his decision to speak; Loris-Melikov's notebook visible only as HM notices it. CF-08, NF-09.

refocalize.scene: HM's memory of his mother showing him the scar
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator rendering HM's interior memory
refocalize.recommended_pov: Keep as HM's subjective flashback — close-up on child's hand touching the scar, no narrator voice. TRANSLATE_TO_VISUAL. NF-04.

refocalize.scene: Arrival of Murid missionaries beating stones — mass conversion of Chechens and Avars
refocalize.current_pov: Hadji Murad narrating to Loris-Melikov (retrospective, distanced)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as HM's direct memory: young HM watching from the palace walls as the Murids arrive below, framed through his eyes — converts an abstract historical statement into a concrete visual memory. NF-09, CF-08.

refocalize.scene: Hamzad sends envoys to the Khans demanding Ghazavat allegiance
refocalize.current_pov: HM narrating at one remove (reported speech within backstory)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Either compress to a single line in HM's dictation or restage as the envoy arriving at the palace while young HM watches — keeps it in his POV. CF-08.

refocalize.scene: Umma Khan's corruption by Russian officers (cards, wine, bad places)
refocalize.current_pov: Hadji Murad narrating retrospectively to Loris-Melikov
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as witnessed scene from Hadji Murad's POV — he watches Umma Khan's degradation helplessly, then physically removes him; converts oral report into visual scene with dramatic agency

refocalize.scene: Hamzad's humiliation of the Khans' Elders
refocalize.current_pov: Reported speech via Hadji Murad's narration
refocalize.recommended_pov: Brief visual insert (30 seconds) showing the Elders' return to Khunzakh with shaved faces and cakes — wordless, kinetic; then cut back to HM's reaction; CF-08 refokalisation

refocalize.scene: Bulach Khan sent as hostage to Hamzad
refocalize.recommended_pov: Can remain narrated if the Khansha is present in the scene as a listener or interlocutor — her emotional weight carries the beat without POV switch

refocalize.scene: Khansha shaming Abu Nutsal Khan into riding to Hamzad
refocalize.current_pov: Hadji Murad retrospective narration (he was present but the emotional core is the Khansha-Abu Nutsal exchange)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Keep as HM's POV — he watches the shaming, recognizes the fatal dynamic, is powerless to stop it. Restage as HM in the doorway, witnessing the moment the Khansha speaks the bee-sting line. His face carries the dread. CF-08 / NF-09.

refocalize.scene: Hamzad's theatrical reception of Umma Khan (singing, shooting, caracoling)
refocalize.current_pov: HM's narration — he is present but observing Hamzad's performance directed at Umma Khan
refocalize.recommended_pov: Film through HM's eyes: he watches Hamzad perform honor-as-theater, reads the performance as false. His recognition of the trap is the dramaturgical point. CF-08.

refocalize.scene: Shamil cutting off Hadji Murad at Hamzad's tent
refocalize.current_pov: HM's retrospective narration (already his POV — no change needed)
refocalize.recommended_pov: KEEP as HM POV — this is already correctly focalized. The moment is the seed of the HM-Shamil antagonism. High filmability. NF-09.

refocalize.scene: Abu Nutsal Khan's massacre in Hamzad's tent
refocalize.recommended_pov: HM's POV as direct witness — film the memory as fragmented flash-cuts from HM's perspective (running away, sound of shots, glimpsed carnage), then cut back to his face in the present. Avoids VO; keeps shame visceral. CF-08 / NF-04.

refocalize.scene: Loris-Melikov observing murids in their room
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as HM returning from prayer and seeing Loris-Melikov emerge from the murid room — HM reads Gamzalo's shut face, understands his household is being studied. Refocalises on protagonist, adds dramatic irony.

refocalize.scene: Loris-Melikov visits murids for debate on Shamil/Mansur
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as scene Hadji Murad overhears from his adjacent bedroom — he would hear his men debate the legitimacy of the world he left behind, adding dramatic irony to his captive situation → CF-08, NF-09

refocalize.scene: Loris-Melikov profiles the murids — including reading Gamzalo as potential spy
refocalize.recommended_pov: Hadji Murad — restage as HM watching Loris-Melikov watch Gamzalo; HM reads the Russian's suspicion and registers its danger. CF-08 applies: keep the information, shift the lens.

refocalize.scene: Loris-Melikov's interior monologue suspecting HM of espionage while observing Gamzalo
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as HM overhearing / sensing the Russian's suspicion — or cut the interior narration entirely and show suspicion via Loris-Melikov's body language in the drawing-room scene that follows. CF-08: refocalise onto HM as primary POV anchor.

refocalize.scene: Loris-Melikov's interrogation of Khanefi in the side room
refocalize.recommended_pov: Film can keep this scene — Loris-Melikov POV is acceptable here as it delivers Khanefi's backstory efficiently. Alternatively, HM could relay Khanefi's story himself in the Chapter XIII narration session, collapsing both scenes.

refocalize.scene: Hamzad kills the Khansha via Aseldar
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator / HM recounting — HM was not present
refocalize.recommended_pov: Keep as HM oral testimony to Loris-Melikov — i.e. hearsay, not witnessed. Film renders as a brief flash-cut insert seen through HM's imagination/telling, then returns to drawing room. This preserves HM as filter of all information per CF-08/NF-09.

refocalize.scene: Grandfather receives Hamzad's threat and reports back
refocalize.current_pov: Third-person narration within HM's account — HM absent from Hamzad's palace scene
refocalize.recommended_pov: Compress to a single line of HM dialogue: 'Our grandfather came home and told us.' No visual insert needed — SC-04 ellipsis cut.

refocalize.scene: Loris-Melikov's interpolated question 'Why didst thou not go with him?'
refocalize.recommended_pov: Keep as HM direct address to camera/implied listener; Loris-Melikov's question can become a written title card or HM's own rhetorical pause — no separate interviewer POV needed (CF-08, NF-09)

refocalize.scene: General Klugenau departing for Tiflis and Akhmet Khan acting in his absence
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as HM watching soldiers arrive — he need not know the politics behind it; institutional betrayal shown through its effect on him (CF-08)

refocalize.scene: Klugenau letter reading by Loris-Melikov
refocalize.current_pov: Loris-Melikov (reader of document, observer of Hadji Murad)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Hadji Murad — restage as HM physically handing over the letters, his face and crooked-leg gesture carrying the emotional weight; Loris-Melikov's reading becomes background action

refocalize.scene: Klugenau letter read aloud to Loris-Melikov
refocalize.recommended_pov: Hadji Murad — restage as HM's memory: brief visual flashback of receiving the letter, looking at it, setting it aside untrusted. Eliminates Loris-Melikov as passive relay, centers HM's internal calculus.

refocalize.scene: Military exploits narration (digest)
refocalize.current_pov: Authorial summary outside any character's POV
refocalize.recommended_pov: CUT — no restaging possible for pure digest prose. If beat is needed, a single line of HM's dialogue to Loris-Melikov suffices.

refocalize.scene: Vorontsov writes to Chernyshov reporting HM's situation
refocalize.recommended_pov: Refocalize through Hadji Murad — he is unaware of how the Russians are framing him; dramatic irony if shown as parallel intercut or translated to a scene HM later learns of via Butler or Loris-Melikov

refocalize.scene: Hadji Murad's life story summary (raids, Shamil relationship, Tabasaran)
refocalize.current_pov: Narrator summarizes HM's account ('there were very many and some of which were already familiar to Loris-Melikov')
refocalize.recommended_pov: Already HM's POV in dialogue; film should dramatize only the Shamil-betrayal beat directly, compress the raid catalogue to a single visual montage or a line of dialogue — NF-04 narrator summary must not survive as VO

refocalize.scene: Loris-Melikov's written report narrating HM's daily habits (sleeplessness, prayer, riding, daily visits)
refocalize.current_pov: Loris-Melikov epistolary — third-person report on HM
refocalize.recommended_pov: Direct scene: HM at night in his quarters, restless; morning ride with Cossack escort; arrival at Loris-Melikov's office. All visual, no narrator. CF-08 / NF-04.

refocalize.scene: Loris-Melikov's frank warning about Shamil's likely response (family threatened/killed)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Direct confrontation scene between HM and Loris-Melikov — the warning delivered face to face, HM's response (arms to heaven) played as a close-up beat. SC-03 value-shift (hope → fatalistic resolve) must be preserved.

refocalize.scene: Loris-Melikov's report to Vorontsov on HM's demands and the political dilemma of how to treat him
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as direct scene between Hadji Murad and Loris-Melikov — HM's plea visible in real time; Loris-Melikov's dilemma expressed through behavior/silence rather than letter prose → CF-08, NF-04

refocalize.scene: Vorontsov's letter outlining Hadji Murat's constraints and escorts
refocalize.recommended_pov: Hadji Murat experiences the constraints directly — Cossack escort assembled, told he cannot go to Vozdvizhensk, presented to Kozlovsky. Show the cage, not the paperwork. CF-08, NF-04

refocalize.scene: Vorontsov's letter closing paragraphs (Tarkhanov assessment of HM)
refocalize.current_pov: Epistolary — Vorontsov writing to Prince Chernyshov; auktorial narrator frames the delivery
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as a brief spoken exchange between Vorontsov and Tarkhanov (or Loris-Melikov if fused), overheard or witnessed through Hadji Murad's partial understanding — CF-08, NF-09

refocalize.scene: Courier ride to St. Petersburg / New Year's Eve delivery
refocalize.recommended_pov: DISSOLVE or title card (SC-04 ellipsis cut); brief insert of Chernyshov receiving sealed document suffices — no POV character needed

refocalize.scene: Chernyshov's plan to undermine Vorontsov in his New Year's report
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator (Tolstoi explains Chernyshov's intent and its failure retrospectively)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as Hadji Murad learns the outcome — a messenger or Loris-Melikov reports that his transfer to Central Russia was proposed but failed; HM's relief/uncertainty is the dramatic beat. CF-08, NF-04.

refocalize.scene: Nicholas's ill temper causing Chernyshov's plan to fail
refocalize.current_pov: Authorial omniscience — narrator tells us Nicholas's inner state and reasoning
refocalize.recommended_pov: Show through Chernyshov's deflated exit from the audience chamber, readable without inner access. NF-04, NF-09.

refocalize.scene: Chernyshov's palace arrival and anteroom wait
refocalize.recommended_pov: Compress into the Nicholas audience scene already anchored in prior chapters; OR show only the result of the audience as reported to Hadji Murat via Loris-Melikov — CF-08, NF-09

refocalize.scene: Nicholas's cabinet audience with Chernyshov and Dolgoruky
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator — satirical description of Nicholas from outside
refocalize.recommended_pov: Chernyshov's restrained observer POV — we read Nicholas's mood through Chernyshov's careful calculation of how to pitch the Hadji Murad report. Keeps HM's fate as the dramatic engine even in the Nicholas chapter. Rule: CF-08, NF-04, NF-09.

refocalize.scene: Masquerade pursuit of the masked woman
refocalize.recommended_pov: CUT — or compress to a single physical detail (dark circles, rumpled uniform collar) glimpsed by Chernyshov as he enters. The masquerade need not be shown at all. Rule: SS-04, SC-01.

refocalize.scene: Entire Nicholas masquerade and seduction sequence
refocalize.recommended_pov: Compress to a single scene visible from the perspective of a Russian officer subordinate to Vorontsov — Nicholas's depravity reaches the Caucasus command structure as rumour or report, reframing his imperial decisions on Hadji Murad's fate against this background of moral vacuum. Alternatively: cut entirely and let Nicholas's chapter 15 cabinet scene (already in state) carry his characterisation. CF-08 + NF-04.

refocalize.scene: Nicholas's bedroom interior monologue (Napoleon cloak)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Translate to visual: cloak draped on hard bed, Nicholas staring at ceiling — no voice-over. NF-04: auktorialer Erzähler auflösen. SC-06: translate to visual.

refocalize.scene: Entire chunk — Nicholas I morning walk and self-justification
refocalize.current_pov: Nicholas I (deep internal free indirect discourse)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as external observation: a palace servant or aide watches Nicholas on the embankment from a distance; Nicholas's grotesque self-importance becomes visible behavior rather than sympathetic interiority. OR: cut entirely and compress into the bureaucratic scene where his decision on Hadji Murat arrives — his indifference is the point, not his inner life. CF-08, NF-04.

refocalize.scene: Embankment encounter with Polosatov
refocalize.current_pov: Nicholas I (his perception of the student)
refocalize.recommended_pov: If retained, shoot from Polosatov's POV — terrified young man facing an enormous uniformed emperor who calls him 'Blockhead' for honesty. Converts Nicholas's self-flattering encounter into an image of power's casual cruelty. NF-09, SC-03.

refocalize.scene: Nicholas receives Chernyshov's morning reports including Vorontsov's dispatch on Hadji Murad
refocalize.current_pov: Nicholas I (antagonist) — we are inside his head throughout
refocalize.recommended_pov: KEEP as Nicholas POV but compress: the scene's power is precisely that HM is reduced to a bureaucratic line item. Refokalisation onto HM would destroy this irony. Instead, film this scene from a detached, slightly elevated camera angle — Tolstoi's authorial irony translated into visual distance (NF-04 + NF-05: cut auktorial narrator voice, translate contempt into camera stance). The moment Chernyshov mentions 'Vorontsov's report about Hadji Murad' should land as a beat — perhaps a brief reaction from Nicholas that reveals his disposition.

refocalize.scene: Helena Pavlovna carriage glimpsed from the portico
refocalize.recommended_pov: If retained: KEEP Nicholas POV, but translate his interior class-contempt monologue into behavior — a dismissive turn of the head, an accelerated walk back inside. NF-04: cut auktorial summary of his thoughts, show via action. Alternatively CUT scene entirely (see cuttable above).

refocalize.scene: Nicholas cabinet scene — Chernyshov reports HM's surrender; Nicholas reacts with satisfaction.
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator (Tolstoi's ironic third-person, exposing Nicholas's self-deception)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as a scene Hadji Murad never witnesses but whose consequences reach him via Loris-Melikov's attitude shift. Alternatively: compress to a single insert shot — a dispatch rider arriving at Nicholas's palace — that conveys 'the Tsar has been told' without a full cabinet scene. If kept as scene, play it from Chernyshov's POV to externalize the flattery dynamic without needing narrator irony.

refocalize.scene: Nicholas reviews military strategy with Chernyshov and claims Ermolov's plan as his own
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient authorial narrator exposing Nicholas's psychology directly
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as dramatised scene: Chernyshov flatters Nicholas on-camera; Nicholas's acceptance of contradiction shown behaviourally (nods, signs document) — cut authorial explication; NF-04, CF-08

refocalize.scene: Bzhezovski student case reviewed by Nicholas
refocalize.recommended_pov: CUT entirely or compress to single reaction beat within an existing Nicholas cabinet scene — SC-01, SS-04

refocalize.scene: Nicholas's cabinet — Bzhezovski sentencing + Caucasus policy orders
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator (Tolstoi intrudes directly to condemn Nicholas)
refocalize.recommended_pov: Keep scene in Nicholas's cabinet but strip authorial commentary; translate Tolstoi's irony into visual contrast — Chernyshov's performative bow while Nicholas's misspelled death sentence fills frame. NF-04 applies: auktorialer Erzähler → Kamera-Entscheidung.

refocalize.scene: Nicholas decides Hadji Murad stays in the Caucasus under Vorontsov
refocalize.current_pov: Nicholas's cabinet — omniscient narrator with Nicholas as focaliser
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as HM learning the decision secondhand (via Loris-Melikov or a letter read aloud at Nukha) — keeps HM as narrative centre, converts imperial decree into dramatic news that lands on the protagonist; CF-08 / NF-09.

refocalize.scene: Bibikov's private moral horror at the orders
refocalize.recommended_pov: Cut interiority entirely or translate to a single visible gesture (the bowed grey head) — NF-04 auktorialer Erzähler Auflösung; the image already carries the meaning.

refocalize.scene: Nicholas's New Year court ritual and church attendance
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient auktorial narrator (Tolstoi's ironic third person); Nicholas is the focalizer
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as glimpsed court spectacle — seen briefly through a messenger or courier arriving with dispatches about Hadji Murad. Nicholas's grandiosity becomes backdrop to the bureaucratic machinery that decides HM's fate. Alternatively, compress to a single scene: Nicholas signing HM's restricted-movement order amid New Year festivities, tying the two threads.

refocalize.scene: Nelidova noticed at church; pension ordered for mother
refocalize.recommended_pov: Cut interiority entirely; translate to visual behavior only — Nicholas's gaze lingering, a whispered order to an aide. NF-04 applies: auktorial narrator dissolved, behavior carries the satirical weight.

refocalize.scene: Imperial dinner — Lieven / Prussian Ambassador conversation before Nicholas arrives
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as seen by Chernyshov entering the hall — he witnesses the Ambassador's private glance, registers the gap between flattery and reality; this keeps HM's chain of command in frame → CF-08

refocalize.scene: Nicholas at the ballet, diamond ring to dancer
refocalize.recommended_pov: Cut to Chernyshov waiting in antechamber with the war dispatch — Nicholas exits ballet, signs order without reading it; juxtaposition replaces narrator irony → NF-04, CF-08

refocalize.scene: Chapter XVI opening — January 1852 raid ordered and column marching
refocalize.recommended_pov: Open on the boots of marching soldiers, pull back to reveal the column — no narrator needed; or cut directly to Hadji Murad hearing news of the raid from Butler → CF-08, NF-04

refocalize.scene: January 1852 Chechnya raid column march — Chapter XVI opening
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as Hadji Murad learning of the raid via messenger or observing the column from the hills — keeps protagonist as witness; Butler's romanticism can survive as a brief intercut if budget allows → CF-08

refocalize.scene: Butler's interior monologue about not flinching under fire and carrying his head high
refocalize.recommended_pov: External behavior — show Butler literally straightening his posture, smiling, speaking calmly to soldiers while shells land; cut inner narration per NF-04

refocalize.scene: Chechen horsemen retreating and aoul burning — narrated from Butler's advancing perspective
refocalize.current_pov: Butler following Cossacks into the aoul
refocalize.recommended_pov: KEEP Butler POV but intercut with a silent Chechen family watching from the treeline (invented visual bridge) to activate the thematic echo with Sado's aoul; pivot to HM later learning of the raid → CF-08, NF-04

refocalize.scene: Butler's inner monologue on war romanticism / deliberate avoidance of corpses
refocalize.recommended_pov: TRANSLATE_TO_VISUAL — hold on the waxen hand and dark red spot that Butler's eye skips past; camera sees what he refuses to; no voice-over. Alternatively, restage as a scene Hadji Murad later learns of (his own people's aoul burned), converting Butler's euphoria into HM's grief — CF-08, NF-04

refocalize.scene: Raid on deserted Chechen aoul — burning, looting
refocalize.recommended_pov: Keep as Russian-POV counterpoint sequence (structural contrast to HM's family scenes) but compress to montage; no independent subplot status — SS-09, SC-02

refocalize.scene: Butler's march and domestic arrival at fort
refocalize.recommended_pov: CUT or compress to background beat — HM is absent; scene can survive only as brief atmospheric contrast cut juxtaposed with HM's confinement in Nukha (CF-08, SC-02)

refocalize.scene: Sado's aoul destruction — currently narrated from omniscient authorial POV observing the village
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as Hadji Murad learning of the destruction via report or messenger — converts authorial moral commentary into dramatic blow delivered to protagonist, CF-08/NF-04

refocalize.scene: Chechen aoul communal grief and decision to appeal to Shamil
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator / collective Chechen community POV
refocalize.recommended_pov: Reframe as event Hadji Murad learns of — a messenger arrives at Nukha reporting the aoul's destruction and Shamil's new power over the population; sharpens HM's guilt and stakes of family rescue → CF-08

refocalize.scene: Butler's morning walk and mountain vista
refocalize.recommended_pov: Cut entirely or compress to a single establishing shot of the fort the morning after the raid, seen from outside — no Butler interiority needed → SC-01

refocalize.scene: Butler's pre-arrival reflection on Marya Dmitrievna
refocalize.recommended_pov: Cut or merge into the arrival scene as Butler's distracted manner when he first spots the approaching riders — translate restlessness to visible behavior

refocalize.scene: Hadji Murad's arrival at the fort
refocalize.recommended_pov: KEEP Butler as POV lens — this is the most efficient way to introduce HM to both Butler and the audience simultaneously; no refocalization needed here

refocalize.scene: Marya Dmitrievna's kitchen scene — Butler navigating domestic chaos
refocalize.recommended_pov: Keep Butler as POV lens; Marya Dmitrievna's disbelief can be compressed to one line; HM waiting outside is the dramatic anchor, not the kitchen

refocalize.scene: Petrov waking and grumbling in bedroom
refocalize.recommended_pov: Refocalize to HM's exterior POV — we see the delay from HM's perspective (waiting, watching the disordered fort), Petrov's chaos becomes subtext of Russian dysfunction rather than interior character study; CF-08

refocalize.scene: Marya Dmitrievna's guest-chamber proposal and her averted gaze at HM
refocalize.recommended_pov: Hadji Murad — his noticing of her simplicity and directness becomes the beat; his POV anchors why she registers as trustworthy amid hostile bureaucracy

refocalize.scene: Shamil's triumphant return to Vedeno with murids firing rifles, crowds celebrating
refocalize.recommended_pov: Yusuf in the pit hears the distant shots and singing — the celebration that seals his fate reaches him as muffled thunder; or spy report to Hadji Murad restages it through his imagination

refocalize.scene: Hadji Murad's family in Ibrahim Raschid's saklya awaiting Shamil's decision
refocalize.recommended_pov: Dramatize via spy delivering this information to Hadji Murad at the fort — his face is the screen; audience sees his family through his dread, not directly

refocalize.scene: Shamil's triumphal entry through the aoul
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator / crowd perspective describing Shamil from outside
refocalize.recommended_pov: Yusuf in the pit — he hears the gunshots, the chanting, smells the crowd above; the triumph lands as torture, not spectacle. Restage Shamil's entry as sound+shadow bleeding through the pit grate. SC-03 (value shift: hope→despair for Yusuf) demands this beat be preserved; reframing through Yusuf intensifies it.

refocalize.scene: Shamil's return to Vedeno — full inner-life POV section
refocalize.current_pov: Shamil (intimate third-person, access to his thoughts and desires)
refocalize.recommended_pov: KEEP as Shamil POV for this scene — it is load-bearing as antagonist counter-portrait; restage as parallel montage with HM's confinement in Nukha to make the structural rhyme explicit (CF-08 refokalisation deferred: Shamil IS a named antagonist, his POV justified by M-02 thematic weight)

refocalize.scene: Jemal Eddin's report on HM's family detection and imprisonment
refocalize.current_pov: Shamil (antagonist POV — receiving intelligence about HM's failed family-rescue attempt)
refocalize.recommended_pov: KEEP as Shamil POV — this is one of the few justified antagonist scenes; HM learns of this failure later, making it a dramatic irony scene. Alternatively REFOCAL: show HM in Nukha simultaneously receiving different news, intercut. CF-08 applies if cutting Shamil entirely, but SC-03 (value change: HM's plan → failure) argues for KEEP

refocalize.scene: Shamil's council chamber judicial proceedings
refocalize.recommended_pov: Compress to establishing shot of council authority; refocalize toward the HM decision as the only narratively relevant beat — NF-04 (cut auktorial narrator), SC-01 (compress procedural detail)

refocalize.scene: Shamil's council deliberation on Hadji Murad's fate
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator inside Shamil's council chamber — Hadji Murad is absent
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as information HM receives later (via spy, Eldar, or overheard conversation in Nukha), or cut interior council scene and show only its consequence: the assassin dispatched toward Tiflis. CF-08, NF-04

refocalize.scene: Shamil's prophetic silence ritual
refocalize.current_pov: Narrator explains the meaning; councilors' interpretation given
refocalize.recommended_pov: Keep as visual beat without narrator explanation — let the image (held close-up, waiting men) speak. NF-09, SC-06

refocalize.scene: Shamil's council deliberation and prophetic silence ritual
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as scene HM later learns of through Yusuf's letter or a returning messenger — keeps camera in HM's orbit per CF-08 refocalisation rule

refocalize.scene: Yusuf's inner thoughts about admiring Shamil and resenting his father
refocalize.recommended_pov: Translate to visible behaviour — Yusuf's deep bow, the trembling veneration in his approach — no voice-over; HM reads this in the letter's tone when it arrives (NF-04)

refocalize.scene: Shamil pronounces blinding sentence and Yusuf attempts suicide
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator following Shamil and Yusuf in Vedeno
refocalize.recommended_pov: Reach HM in the fort via messenger/spy report — HM learns of son's blinding verdict and attempted suicide as devastating news; reaction shot is the scene

refocalize.scene: Shamil's nocturnal circuit to Aminal's room
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator in Shamil's domestic quarters
refocalize.recommended_pov: CUT — no HM-refocalisation viable; irony is authorial, not filmable through HM's eyes

refocalize.scene: Khanefi's song performance
refocalize.recommended_pov: Hadji Murad — reframe as HM's internal experience (close-up on closed eyes, the song's words as his unspoken interiority). Butler's reaction becomes background. SC-03 preserved: the value-shift is HM moving from stoic mask to private grief, not Butler's aesthetic appreciation.

refocalize.scene: Butler adopts Circassian dress
refocalize.recommended_pov: If kept: restage as HM observing Butler's costume with an unreadable expression — lets the irony land visually without voicing it. NF-04 applies: auktorial irony translated to image rather than narratorial comment.

refocalize.scene: Officers at the tea-table before HM enters
refocalize.recommended_pov: Hadji Murad's entrance POV — film opens on the room as HM limps in, seeing the officers through his eyes

refocalize.scene: Officers' admiration of the Gurda sword indoors
refocalize.recommended_pov: Cut entirely or hold on Butler watching HM leave — the sword detail surfaces in Butler's dialogue, not a separate interior shot — CF-08, SC-02

refocalize.scene: Arslan Khan's perspective — pale, trembling, going inside with the major
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator shifting briefly to Arslan Khan's humiliation
refocalize.recommended_pov: Stay on HM: his breathing-heavily smile reads Arslan Khan's humiliation without cutting away. CF-08, NF-09.

refocalize.scene: Russian officers' post-departure discussion of Hadji Murad
refocalize.recommended_pov: Retain as-is — HM has just left, scene works as reaction beat. Could optionally be glimpsed as HM rides away and hears distant Russian voices (externalized via sound bridge). CF-08 not strictly required here since HM is the topic even if absent.

refocalize.scene: Baryatinsky's arrival and expedition muster
refocalize.recommended_pov: Compress to a single scene seen from Butler's or Simon Vorontsov's perspective as news arrives, eliminating the omniscient survey mode. HM need not be present; the scene establishes the closing net around his situation.

refocalize.scene: Marya Vasilevna's presence at camp causing night-ambush duty
refocalize.recommended_pov: CUT — HM not present; if any beat survives, restage as Butler observing the absurdity → CF-08

refocalize.scene: Farewell dinner for Kozlovsky
refocalize.recommended_pov: COMPRESS to Butler's POV — one beat of soldiers-playing-society-at-the-front suffices; Kozlovsky toast anxiety becomes a single ironic line → SC-02, CF-08

refocalize.scene: Kozlovsky farewell dinner and speech
refocalize.current_pov: Butler / omniscient narrator observing the officers
refocalize.recommended_pov: CUT — scene not refocalizable through HM; if one Butler-susceptibility beat is needed, MERGE into existing Butler-at-fort scene (SC-02 MERGE). Rule: SS-02 (no theme link), SC-01 (fails all three tests).

refocalize.scene: Butler's card-game collapse and subsequent shame spiral
refocalize.recommended_pov: CUT or reduce to a single reaction shot of Butler returning hollow-eyed to barracks — his disillusionment then functions as atmospheric counterpoint to HM's escape preparations in the parallel storyline (CF-08, NF-04)

refocalize.scene: Vorontsov's bureaucratic deferral pending Argutinski
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator reporting Vorontsov's thinking and administrative process
refocalize.recommended_pov: HM's POV — show him waiting, re-entering the palace daily, reading Vorontsov's face; the delay lands as subjective mounting dread, not administrative procedure. CF-08 applies.

refocalize.scene: Narrator's explicit epistemological gap passage ('For Vorontsov … a lucky turn … For Hadji Murad … a terrible crisis')
refocalize.recommended_pov: TRANSLATE_TO_VISUAL: intercut Russian officers toasting the Caucasian news with HM alone at prayer in a borrowed room — contrast does what the narrator states. NF-04 + SC-06 apply.

refocalize.scene: Avar loyalists' plan to intercept the family en route from Vedeno
refocalize.recommended_pov: Hadji Murad receiving spy's oral report — restaged as tense dialogue scene through HM's eyes; CF-08 / NF-04

refocalize.scene: Kirillov's private aside in Russian ('What does he want with the money?')
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator reveals Kirillov's condescending internal commentary
refocalize.recommended_pov: Stay on HM's face — his comprehension and contemptuous glance IS the scene; the narrator aside becomes redundant when HM's reaction shows he understood → NF-04, CF-08

refocalize.scene: The falcon fable as interior monologue
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator rendering Hadji Murad's thoughts verbatim
refocalize.recommended_pov: HM speaks the fable aloud to himself or to Eldar in hushed dialogue — externalises thought without voice-over, preserves the metaphor cinematically (NF-04, CF-08)

refocalize.scene: Gamzalo's internal state — his long-held desire to kill Russians and flee
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator reporting Gamzalo's hidden feelings
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as HM observing Gamzalo's face lighting up when he receives the order — HM reads what Gamzalo has always wanted; no narration needed. → CF-08, NF-04

refocalize.scene: Khanefi sings Hamzad ballad overheard by HM from the hall
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator describes song content in full verse form
refocalize.recommended_pov: Stay on HM's face in the darkened hall — let the sound design carry the song; intercut with his eyes, the jug, the spill. Ballad lyrics can be partial subtitle, not full recitation.

refocalize.scene: Memory of mother carrying Hadji Murad as child through mountains
refocalize.current_pov: Auktorial — narrator describes HM's inner vision
refocalize.recommended_pov: HM's subjective memory rendered as silent flashback image: young woman, basket, mountain pass — no voice-over, pure visual; NF-04 eliminates auktorial narration, SC-06 translates literary interiority into image

refocalize.scene: Memory of Yusuf's farewell at the stream — his height, daring, the moment he turned back
refocalize.current_pov: Auktorial interiority with physical description catalogue
refocalize.recommended_pov: Brief silent flashback: Yusuf on horseback at stream edge, turns, rides away — HM watches. Cut back to HM's face in Nukha. The blinding threat lands as a title card or HM's whispered repetition of Shamil's words — CF-08 / NF-09

refocalize.scene: Chapter XXIV opening — Butler's moral decline and Baryatinsky's arrival at the fort
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as events Hadji Murad observes or is told of during his dawn ride; or compress to a single intercut — HM riding at dawn / Russian officers drinking themselves numb — visual contrast without Butler's inner monologue → CF-08, NF-04

refocalize.scene: Major Petrov drunk, Butler watching and leaving
refocalize.recommended_pov: CUT — scene has no connection to HM's POV; if retained, compress into single image (drunken officer as background atmosphere in Butler's chapter)

refocalize.scene: Butler and Marya Dmitrievna moonlit walk
refocalize.recommended_pov: CUT or reduce to single beat — Kamenev's arrival is the only load-bearing moment; fold into Kamenev scene directly via CF-08

refocalize.scene: Display of HM's head
refocalize.current_pov: Third-person omniscient, centred on Butler and Marya Dmitrievna as witnesses
refocalize.recommended_pov: KEEP Butler as witness POV — he is the nearest surrogate for audience identification and his kunak-bond with HM gives personal stakes. Marya's exit is the second beat. No refocalisation needed; this is already the optimal cinematic arrangement.

refocalize.scene: Head display scene at Butler's outpost
refocalize.current_pov: Butler / omniscient narrator observing the officers' reaction
refocalize.recommended_pov: Butler — his recognition of HM's face as someone he knew personally is the emotional anchor; all officer reactions filtered through Butler's mounting horror and guilt

refocalize.scene: Marya Dmitrievna's condemnation
refocalize.recommended_pov: Keep as is — her POV is the moral counterpoint; brief cut-away to her on the steps before returning to Butler is cinematically efficient

refocalize.scene: Commander instructs Nazarov to keep close to HM
refocalize.recommended_pov: HM's POV — show him overhearing the order, registering it, deliberately ignoring it; turns passive scene into active character moment → CF-08, NF-09

refocalize.scene: Cossack escort characterizations (Nazarov, Ferapontov, Ignatov, Mishkin, Petrakov)
refocalize.recommended_pov: HM's scanning gaze — he sizes up his escort as potential obstacles; reframes exposition as tactical assessment → CF-08

refocalize.scene: Nazarov's subjective exhilaration passage ('life played so joyously in Nazarov's soul')
refocalize.recommended_pov: TRANSLATE_TO_VISUAL — hold on Nazarov's grinning face in close-up as he closes the gap, then smash-cut to HM's pistol rising. The irony survives visually without narrator intrusion. NF-04 (authorial narrator cut) + SC-06 (translate inner beauty to image).

refocalize.scene: Fort commander receives news of escape and orders pursuit
refocalize.recommended_pov: Restage as a visual consequence seen from HM's retreating position: distant alarm shot, then sound of massing horses — HM hears the empire mobilising; or dissolve to brief Russian reaction insert only → CF-08

refocalize.scene: Mishkin's gallop to the fortress
refocalize.recommended_pov: Show only from HM/Khanefi/Bata POV: they watch him go, know he will raise the alarm, ride harder — no need to follow Mishkin independently → CF-08

refocalize.scene: Nightingale song / Hamzad-memory passage
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator describing HM's inner state and the nightingales
refocalize.recommended_pov: Stay with HM — translate inner reverie to behavior: he stills, closes eyes, lips move with remembered words; nightingales heard on soundtrack as underscore. NF-04 / SC-06.

refocalize.scene: Karganov gathering his militia and searching for HM's trail
refocalize.recommended_pov: Stay with HM in the thicket — encirclement discovered from inside (sounds of hooves, Bata's reconnaissance report) → NF-04, CF-08

refocalize.scene: Old man encounters Karganov and gives directions
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator, away from HM entirely
refocalize.recommended_pov: Cut entirely or show as brief intercut establishing the noose closing; return immediately to HM's POV → CF-08, SC-01

refocalize.scene: Hadji Aga's arrival and sword-charge order
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator describes Hadji Aga's command and the mass charge from outside the entrenchment
refocalize.recommended_pov: Stay inside the entrenchment with HM — he hears the shouts, sees the surge; Hadji Aga's identity revealed through HM's recognition (a face he knows). CF-08, NF-09.

refocalize.scene: Dying vision sequence (Abu Nutsal Khan, Vorontsov, Yusuf, Sofiat, Shamil)
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient narrator describing HM's inner images
refocalize.recommended_pov: HM's subjective POV — rapid visual flash-cuts, no dialogue, no narration; translate inner images to silent montage per NF-04 (cut narrator voice, translate to image/montage)

refocalize.scene: Militiamen triumph and gather around bodies like sportsmen around slaughtered animal
refocalize.recommended_pov: Stay on HM's subjective final consciousness as long as possible, then cut to wide shot of triumphant crowd — the switch in register IS the thematic point (HM has left; only his body remains). SC-03 (value shift: agency → object) must be preserved.

refocalize.scene: Hadji Aga decapitation described in clinical detail
refocalize.recommended_pov: Film can hold on the crowd's faces (Karganov, Akhmet Khan) reacting — the decapitation implied by their expressions and the roll of the head into frame, not shown in full. Keeps moral weight without exploitation. NF-04 applied.

refocalize.scene: Thistle-frame closing sentence
refocalize.recommended_pov: Pure visual: ECU on crushed thistle in ploughed field, no voice-over. NF-04 + SC-06 + NF-08 (closing echo with thematic progression = permitted).

refocalize.scene: Closing narrator frame — thistle and nightingales
refocalize.current_pov: Omniscient auktorial narrator (first-person intrusion: 'I was reminded')
refocalize.recommended_pov: Pure visual: close-up of crushed thistle in ploughed field, ambient birdsong — no voice, no narrator

direct_quotes: But what energy and tenacity!  With what det
But what energy and tenacity!  With what determination it defended itself, and how dearly it sold its life!
Ah, what a destructive creature is man....How many different plant-lives he destroys to support his own existence!
Man has conquered everything and destroyed millions of plants, yet this one won't submit.
What vitality! Man has conquered everything and destroyed millions of plants, yet this one won't submit.

gathered a large nosegay of summer flowers

climbed down into the ditch to pick the thistle
drove away a bumble-bee sleeping inside a flower
wrapped a handkerchief around his hand to avoid pricks
struggled for nearly five minutes to break the tough stalk
threw the plucked thistle away because it did not fit the nosegay
Threw away the plucked thistle flower, feeling sorry for having destroyed it in vain
Walked home across freshly ploughed black-earth fields

Observed the mutilated but still-standing thistle in detail
Remembered a Caucasian episode from years ago, partly witnessed, partly heard from eye-witnesses

Remembers a Caucasian episode from years ago
Frames the coming narrative as partly witnessed, partly heard from eye-witnesses, and partly imagined

intended to place the thistle in the center of his nosegay
found the task of plucking the thistle surprisingly difficult
felt the thistle no longer seemed fresh or beautiful once plucked
felt the thistle's coarseness was out of place among delicate blossoms
felt sorry for having vainly destroyed a flower that looked beautiful in its proper place
admires the thistle's energy and tenacity (exclamation 'But what energy and tenacity!')
Regret over having vainly destroyed a beautiful flower
Admiration for the thistle's energy, tenacity, and determination
Melancholy reflection on man as a destructive creature
Awareness of the lifelessness of the ploughed field
Awe at the resilience and refusal to submit of the surviving thistle
Triggered recollection — the thistle becomes the associative bridge to the Caucasian story he is about to tell
Admiration for resilient, unsubmitting life
Contemplative, associative mind that moves from the plant to a remembered historical episode
Awareness that the episode has been shaped by his own memory and imagination
social_markers: first-person authorial narrator
educated, literary register with detailed botanical vocabulary
rural/agrarian setting awareness (hay harvest, rye reaping)
uses regional term 'Tartar' for the crimson thistle, signaling local/Russian provincial knowledge
19th-century Russian literary voice
Educated, reflective 19th-century Russian voice
Landed-gentry sensibility — observes a proprietor's ploughed fields with cultivated detachment
Philosophical-moralist register (man vs. nature)
Literary, elevated prose with rhetorical exclamations
Positions himself as eyewitness/chronicler — partly seen, partly heard
Educated, reflective first-person narrator
Russian authorial voice with knowledge of the Caucasus
Literary register, philosophical tone

relationship_dynamics.quality: initial aesthetic desire to possess it, then struggle, then regret and admiration for its resilience — foreshadows symbolic link to Hadji Murad
contemplative, observant, reverent
shares their regional knowledge (calling it 'Tartar'), aware of their mowing customs
Reverent, anthropomorphizing — treats the plant as a wounded warrior with body, arm, bowels, eye
Critical, accusatory — frames man as destroyer of plant-lives
Distant, observational — notes the scale of tilled land without personal engagement
Framing narrator — the thistle's resilience triggers the memory that becomes the tale
admiring, projects heroic quality of resistance onto it
mediates and frames his story; positions himself as witness/curator

new_info: Narrator is a first-person 'I' who frames the tale through a personal experience in the countryside
Setting established: midsummer, hay harvest finished, rye reaping beginning — rural Russia
Narrator introduces the central symbolic motif: the crimson 'Tartar' thistle, which resists destruction and admires energy/tenacity — the frame device that will link to Hadji Murad
Narrator demonstrates a reflective, aesthetic, moralizing sensibility
Chunk cuts off mid-exclamation, introducing the thematic keywords 'energy and tenacity'
The thistle encounter functions as the frame-narrative trigger for the entire Hadji Murad story
Narrator explicitly positions the coming tale as a 'Caucasian episode of years ago'
Narrator's source-claim: partly personal eyewitness, partly second-hand from eye-witnesses — establishes hybrid testimonial authority
Introduces the governing metaphor of the novella: the mutilated-but-unsubmitting 'Tartar' thistle as emblem of Hadji Murad
Narrator's worldview: pessimistic about human destructiveness, but drawn to singular vitality that resists it
Explicitly admits the episode is a hybrid of personal observation, second-hand reports, and imagination
Anchors the coming narrative in late 1851 and in the Caucasus

direct_quotes: My murid.  Eldar is his name
speech_descriptors: asked Hadji Murad, addressing the old man
actions: Rides into Makhmet, a hostile Chechen aoul, on a cold November evening in late 1851
Travels as a fugitive with only one murid, wrapped in hood and burka with a rifle protruding
Tries to attract as little attention as possible
Peers with quick black eyes into the faces of passers-by
Avoids the main road to the open square, turns left into a narrow side street
Stops at the second saklya, cut into the hillside, and looks round
Touches the man lying on the roof with the handle of his leather-plaited whip and clicks his tongue to rouse him
touched the old man with the handle of his leather-plaited whip
clicked his tongue to rouse the old man
uncovered his face when greeting
dismounted
walked with a slight limp
entered under the penthouse
took off his burka, rifle, and sword
handed his weapons to the old man
takes off his burka, rifle, and sword and hands them to the old man
adjusts the pistol at his back
comes up to the cushions
wraps his Circassian coat closer round him and sits down
mirrors the old man's prayer posture — closes eyes, lifts hands palms upwards
repeats a prayer and strokes his face, palms joining at the end of his beard
understands the old man does not wish to say what he knows
slightly nods his head and asks no more questions
introduces his murid Eldar to the old man

The only news is that the hares keep discussing how to drive away the eagles, and the eagles tear first one and then another of them. The other day the Russian dogs burnt the hay in the Mitchit aoul....May their faces be torn!
